verses.mistral: Following Jesus from that place, He saw a man named Matthew sitting at the tax collector's booth, and He said to him, "Follow Me," and getting up, he followed Him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And as Jesus passed forth from thence, he saw a man, named Matthew, sitting at the receipt of custom: and he saith unto him, Follow me. And he arose, and followed him.

verses.mistral: And it came to pass, that as Jesus sat at the table in the house, behold, many sinners and tax collectors came and sat down with Him and His disciples.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, as Jesus sat at meat in the house, behold, many publicans and sinners came and sat down with him and his disciples.

verses.greek: καὶ ἰδόντες οἱ Φαρισαῖοι ⸀ἔλεγον τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ· Διὰ τί μετὰ τῶν τελωνῶν καὶ ἁμαρτωλῶν ἐσθίει ὁ διδάσκαλος ὑμῶν;
verses.mistral: The Pharisees said to His disciples, "Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?"
verses.kjv: And when the Pharisees saw it, they said unto his disciples, Why eateth your Master with publicans and sinners?

verses.greek: ὁ ⸀δὲ ἀκούσας ⸀εἶπεν· Οὐ χρείαν ἔχουσιν οἱ ἰσχύοντες ἰατροῦ ἀλλὰ οἱ κακῶς ἔχοντες.
verses.mistral: Healthy people don't need a doctor, but the sick do.
verses.kjv: But when Jesus heard that, he said unto them, They that be whole need not a physician, but they that are sick.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: πορευθέντες δὲ μάθετε τί ἐστιν· Ἔλεος θέλω καὶ οὐ θυσίαν· οὐ γὰρ ἦλθον καλέσαι δικαίους ἀλλὰ ⸀ἁμαρτωλούς.
verses.mistral: Coming, they learned what this meant: I desire mercy and not sacrifice; for I did not come to call the righteous but sinners.
verses.kjv: But go ye and learn what that meaneth, I will have mercy, and not sacrifice: for I am not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Τότε προσέρχονται αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ Ἰωάννου λέγοντες· Διὰ τί ἡμεῖς καὶ οἱ Φαρισαῖοι νηστεύομεν ⸀πολλά, οἱ δὲ μαθηταί σου οὐ νηστεύουσιν;
verses.mistral: Then come to him the disciples of John saying, "Why do we and the Pharisees fast often, but your disciples do not fast?"
verses.kjv: ¶ Then came to him the disciples of John, saying, Why do we and the Pharisees fast oft, but thy disciples fast not?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς· Μὴ δύνανται οἱ υἱοὶ τοῦ νυμφῶνος πενθεῖν ἐφ’ ὅσον μετ’ αὐτῶν ἐστιν ὁ νυμφίος; ἐλεύσονται δὲ ἡμέραι ὅταν ἀπαρθῇ ἀπ’ αὐτῶν ὁ νυμφίος, καὶ τότε νηστεύσουσιν.
verses.mistral: And Jesus said to them, "Can the bridesmaids mourn as long as the bridegroom is with them? But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast."
verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber mourn, as long as the bridegroom is with them? but the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken from them, and then shall they fast.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: οὐδεὶς δὲ ἐπιβάλλει ἐπίβλημα ῥάκους ἀγνάφου ἐπὶ ἱματίῳ παλαιῷ· αἴρει γὰρ τὸ πλήρωμα αὐτοῦ ἀπὸ τοῦ ἱματίου, καὶ χεῖρον σχίσμα γίνεται.
verses.mistral: A new patch with unshrunk cloth sewn on an old garment will tear the garment and make a worse hole.
verses.kjv: No man putteth a piece of new cloth unto an old garment, for that which is put in to fill it up taketh from the garment, and the rent is made worse.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I throw upon, lay, strike upon
on, to, against, on the basis of, at
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: οὐδὲ βάλλουσιν οἶνον νέον εἰς ἀσκοὺς παλαιούς· εἰ δὲ μή γε, ῥήγνυνται οἱ ἀσκοί, καὶ ὁ οἶνος ἐκχεῖται καὶ οἱ ἀσκοὶ ⸀ἀπόλλυνται· ἀλλὰ βάλλουσιν οἶνον νέον εἰς ἀσκοὺς καινούς, καὶ ἀμφότεροι συντηροῦνται.
verses.mistral: New wine is not put into old wineskins; otherwise, the skins burst, and both the wine and the skins are ruined. But new wine is put into fresh wineskins, and both are preserved.
verses.kjv: Neither do men put new wine into old bottles: else the bottles break, and the wine runneth out, and the bottles perish: but they put new wine into new bottles, and both are preserved.

words.gloss: I cast, throw, rush, put, place, drop
but, on the other hand, and
I cast, throw, rush, put, place, drop
I preserve, keep safe, keep in mind

verses.greek: Ταῦτα αὐτοῦ λαλοῦντος αὐτοῖς ἰδοὺ ἄρχων εἷς ⸀ἐλθὼν προσεκύνει αὐτῷ λέγων ὅτι Ἡ θυγάτηρ μου ἄρτι ἐτελεύτησεν· ἀλλὰ ἐλθὼν ἐπίθες τὴν χεῖρά σου ἐπ’ αὐτήν, καὶ ζήσεται.
verses.mistral: He came to Jesus and fell down before Him, saying, "My daughter has just died; but come and lay Your hand on her, and she will live."
verses.kjv: ¶ While he spake these things unto them, behold, there came a certain ruler, and worshipped him, saying, My daughter is even now dead: but come and lay thy hand upon her, and she shall live.

on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: καὶ ἐγερθεὶς ὁ Ἰησοῦς ⸀ἠκολούθει αὐτῷ καὶ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: And Jesus rose up and followed him, and His disciples.
verses.kjv: And Jesus arose, and followed him, and so did his disciples.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἰδοὺ γυνὴ αἱμορροοῦσα δώδεκα ἔτη προσελθοῦσα ὄπισθεν ἥψατο τοῦ κρασπέδου τοῦ ἱματίου αὐτοῦ·
verses.mistral: Touching the hem of his garment (In response to: " ")
verses.kjv: ¶ And, behold, a woman, which was diseased with an issue of blood twelve years, came behind him, and touched the hem of his garment:

words.gloss: I suffer from a continual flow (oozing) of blood

verses.greek: ἔλεγεν γὰρ ἐν ἑαυτῇ· Ἐὰν μόνον ἅψωμαι τοῦ ἱματίου αὐτοῦ σωθήσομαι.
verses.mistral: Said to herself, "If only I may touch his robe, I shall be saved."
verses.kjv: For she said within herself, If I may but touch his garment, I shall be whole.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς ⸀στραφεὶς καὶ ἰδὼν αὐτὴν εἶπεν· Θάρσει, θύγατερ· ἡ πίστις σου σέσωκέν σε. καὶ ἐσώθη ἡ γυνὴ ἀπὸ τῆς ὥρας ἐκείνης.
verses.mistral: Jesus turned, and seeing her said, "Take heart, daughter; your faith has made you well." And the woman was healed from that hour.
verses.kjv: But Jesus turned him about, and when he saw her, he said, Daughter, be of good comfort; thy faith hath made thee whole. And the woman was made whole from that hour.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ ἐλθὼν ὁ Ἰησοῦς εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν τοῦ ἄρχοντος καὶ ἰδὼν τοὺς αὐλητὰς καὶ τὸν ὄχλον θορυβούμενον
verses.mistral: And coming into the centurion's house, Jesus saw the flute players and the crowd in an uproar
verses.kjv: And when Jesus came into the ruler's house, and saw the minstrels and the people making a noise,

words.gloss: I disturb greatly, terrify, strike with panic

verses.greek: ⸀ἔλεγεν· Ἀναχωρεῖτε, οὐ γὰρ ἀπέθανεν τὸ κοράσιον ἀλλὰ καθεύδει· καὶ κατεγέλων αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Jesus said, "Go away, for the girl has not died but is asleep. And they laughed at him."
verses.kjv: He said unto them, Give place: for the maid is not dead, but sleepeth. And they laughed him to scorn.

words.gloss: I am dying, am about to die

verses.greek: ὅτε δὲ ἐξεβλήθη ὁ ὄχλος, εἰσελθὼν ἐκράτησεν τῆς χειρὸς αὐτῆς, καὶ ἠγέρθη τὸ κοράσιον.
verses.mistral: The girl arose.
verses.kjv: But when the people were put forth, he went in, and took her by the hand, and the maid arose.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ ἐξῆλθεν ἡ φήμη αὕτη εἰς ὅλην τὴν γῆν ἐκείνην.
verses.mistral: This message spread throughout the whole land there.
verses.kjv: And the fame hereof went abroad into all that land.

verses.greek: Καὶ παράγοντι ἐκεῖθεν τῷ Ἰησοῦ ἠκολούθησαν ⸀αὐτῷ δύο τυφλοὶ κράζοντες καὶ λέγοντες· Ἐλέησον ἡμᾶς, ⸀υἱὲ Δαυίδ.
verses.mistral: Following Jesus from there were two blind men crying out and saying, "Have mercy on us, Son of David."
verses.kjv: ¶ And when Jesus departed thence, two blind men followed him, crying, and saying, Thou Son of David, have mercy on us.

words.gloss: I pass by, depart, pass away

verses.greek: ἐλθόντι δὲ εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν προσῆλθον αὐτῷ οἱ τυφλοί, καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς· Πιστεύετε ὅτι δύναμαι τοῦτο ποιῆσαι; λέγουσιν αὐτῷ· Ναί, κύριε.
verses.mistral: The blind men approached when they came into the house, and Jesus said to them, "Do you believe that I can do this?" They said to Him, "Yes, Lord."
verses.kjv: And when he was come into the house, the blind men came to him: and Jesus saith unto them, Believe ye that I am able to do this? They said unto him, Yea, Lord.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: τότε ἥψατο τῶν ὀφθαλμῶν αὐτῶν λέγων· Κατὰ τὴν πίστιν ὑμῶν γενηθήτω ὑμῖν.
verses.mistral: Then He touched their eyes, saying, "According to your faith let it be done to you."
verses.kjv: Then touched he their eyes, saying, According to your faith be it unto you.

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: καὶ ἠνεῴχθησαν αὐτῶν οἱ ὀφθαλμοί. καὶ ⸀ἐνεβριμήθη αὐτοῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς λέγων· Ὁρᾶτε μηδεὶς γινωσκέτω·
verses.mistral: And their eyes were opened. And Jesus said unto them, Let no man know.
verses.kjv: And their eyes were opened; and Jesus straitly charged them, saying, See that no man know it.

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ ἐξελθόντες διεφήμισαν αὐτὸν ἐν ὅλῃ τῇ γῇ ἐκείνῃ.
verses.mistral: They went out and spread the news about Him throughout all that land.
verses.kjv: But they, when they were departed, spread abroad his fame in all that country.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.mistral: Approaching them, they brought to Him a mute demon-possessed man.
verses.kjv: ¶ As they went out, behold, they brought to him a dumb man possessed with a devil.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ ἐκβληθέντος τοῦ δαιμονίου ἐλάλησεν ὁ κωφός. καὶ ἐθαύμασαν οἱ ὄχλοι λέγοντες· Οὐδέποτε ἐφάνη οὕτως ἐν τῷ Ἰσραήλ.
verses.mistral: The mute man began to speak, and the crowd marveled, saying, "Never was anything like this seen in Israel."
verses.kjv: And when the devil was cast out, the dumb spake: and the multitudes marvelled, saying, It was never so seen in Israel.

words.gloss: I throw, cast, put out, banish, bring forth, produce

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ Φαρισαῖοι ἔλεγον· Ἐν τῷ ἄρχοντι τῶν δαιμονίων ἐκβάλλει τὰ δαιμόνια.
verses.mistral: The Pharisees said, "He casts out demons by the prince of demons."
verses.kjv: But the Pharisees said, He casteth out devils through the prince of the devils.

verses.greek: Καὶ περιῆγεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς τὰς πόλεις πάσας καὶ τὰς κώμας, διδάσκων ἐν ταῖς συναγωγαῖς αὐτῶν καὶ κηρύσσων τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τῆς βασιλείας καὶ θεραπεύων πᾶσαν νόσον καὶ πᾶσαν ⸀μαλακίαν.
verses.mistral: Jesus traversed all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every disease and affliction.
verses.kjv: And Jesus went about all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every sickness and every disease among the people.

words.gloss: I care for, attend, serve, heal

verses.greek: Ἰδὼν δὲ τοὺς ὄχλους ἐσπλαγχνίσθη περὶ αὐτῶν ὅτι ἦσαν ἐσκυλμένοι καὶ ἐρριμμένοι ⸀ὡσεὶ πρόβατα μὴ ἔχοντα ποιμένα.
verses.mistral: Feeling compassion for the crowd, because they were harassed and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
verses.kjv: ¶ But when he saw the multitudes, he was moved with compassion on them, because they fainted, and were scattered abroad, as sheep having no shepherd.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
as if, as it were, like, about

verses.greek: τότε λέγει τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ· Ὁ μὲν θερισμὸς πολύς, οἱ δὲ ἐργάται ὀλίγοι·
verses.mistral: The harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few.
verses.kjv: Then saith he unto his disciples, The harvest truly is plenteous, but the labourers are few;

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: δεήθητε οὖν τοῦ κυρίου τοῦ θερισμοῦ ὅπως ἐκβάλῃ ἐργάτας εἰς τὸν θερισμὸν αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Pray then to the Lord of the harvest, that He may send workers into His harvest.
verses.kjv: Pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he will send forth labourers into his harvest.

words.gloss: I throw, cast, put out, banish, bring forth, produce

verses.greek: Καὶ προσκαλεσάμενος τοὺς δώδεκα μαθητὰς αὐτοῦ ἔδωκεν αὐτοῖς ἐξουσίαν πνευμάτων ἀκαθάρτων ὥστε ἐκβάλλειν αὐτὰ καὶ θεραπεύειν πᾶσαν νόσον καὶ πᾶσαν μαλακίαν.
verses.mistral: And calling to Him His twelve disciples, He gave them power over unclean spirits, that they might cast them out and heal every sickness and every disease.
verses.kjv: And when he had called unto him his twelve disciples, he gave them power against unclean spirits, to cast them out, and to heal all manner of sickness and all manner of disease.

I care for, attend, serve, heal

verses.greek: τῶν δὲ δώδεκα ἀποστόλων τὰ ὀνόματά ἐστιν ταῦτα· πρῶτος Σίμων ὁ λεγόμενος Πέτρος καὶ Ἀνδρέας ὁ ἀδελφὸς ⸀αὐτοῦ, Ἰάκωβος ὁ τοῦ Ζεβεδαίου καὶ Ἰωάννης ὁ ἀδελφὸς αὐτοῦ,
verses.mistral: The names of the twelve apostles are as follows: First is Simon called Peter and Andrew his brother, James son of Zebedee and John his brother.
verses.kjv: Now the names of the twelve apostles are these; The first, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother; James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother;

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Φίλιππος καὶ Βαρθολομαῖος, Θωμᾶς καὶ Μαθθαῖος ὁ τελώνης, Ἰάκωβος ὁ τοῦ Ἁλφαίου καὶ ⸀Θαδδαῖος,
verses.mistral: Philip and Bartholomew, Thomas called the Twin, and James son of Alphaeus
verses.kjv: Philip, and Bartholomew; Thomas, and Matthew the publican; James the son of Alphaeus, and Lebbaeus, whose surname was Thaddaeus;

verses.greek: Σίμων ὁ ⸀Καναναῖος καὶ Ἰούδας ⸀ὁ Ἰσκαριώτης ὁ καὶ παραδοὺς αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: Simon the Cananean and Judas Iscariot, the one who betrayed him.
verses.kjv: Simon the Canaanite, and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed him.

verses.greek: Τούτους τοὺς δώδεκα ἀπέστειλεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς παραγγείλας αὐτοῖς λέγων· Εἰς ὁδὸν ἐθνῶν μὴ ἀπέλθητε καὶ εἰς πόλιν Σαμαριτῶν μὴ εἰσέλθητε·
verses.mistral: Do not go to the roads of the Gentiles, and do not enter a city of the Samaritans.
verses.kjv: These twelve Jesus sent forth, and commanded them, saying, Go not into the way of the Gentiles, and into any city of the Samaritans enter ye not:

verses.greek: πορεύεσθε δὲ μᾶλλον πρὸς τὰ πρόβατα τὰ ἀπολωλότα οἴκου Ἰσραήλ.
verses.mistral: Go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.
verses.kjv: But go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: πορευόμενοι δὲ κηρύσσετε λέγοντες ὅτι Ἤγγικεν ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν.
verses.mistral: Approaching, you proclaim saying that the kingdom of heaven is at hand.
verses.kjv: And as ye go, preach, saying, The kingdom of heaven is at hand.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἀσθενοῦντας θεραπεύετε, ⸂νεκροὺς ἐγείρετε⸃, λεπροὺς καθαρίζετε, δαιμόνια ἐκβάλλετε· δωρεὰν ἐλάβετε, δωρεὰν δότε.
verses.mistral: Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse the lepers, cast out demons. Freely you have received, freely give.
verses.kjv: Heal the sick, cleanse the lepers, raise the dead, cast out devils: freely ye have received, freely give.

verses.mistral: Do not acquire gold nor silver nor brass for your belts,
verses.kjv: Provide neither gold, nor silver, nor brass in your purses,

verses.greek: μὴ πήραν εἰς ὁδὸν μηδὲ δύο χιτῶνας μηδὲ ὑποδήματα μηδὲ ⸀ῥάβδον· ἄξιος γὰρ ὁ ἐργάτης τῆς τροφῆς ⸀αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Not two coats, nor bread, nor staff; for the laborer is worthy of his food.
verses.kjv: Nor scrip for your journey, neither two coats, neither shoes, nor yet staves: for the workman is worthy of his meat.

verses.greek: εἰς ἣν δ’ ἂν πόλιν ἢ κώμην εἰσέλθητε, ἐξετάσατε τίς ἐν αὐτῇ ἄξιός ἐστιν· κἀκεῖ μείνατε ἕως ἂν ἐξέλθητε.
verses.mistral: Enter a city or town, and ask who is worthy; there stay until you leave.
verses.kjv: And into whatsoever city or town ye shall enter, enquire who in it is worthy; and there abide till ye go thence.

verses.mistral: Greet the household as you enter
verses.kjv: And when ye come into an house, salute it.

words.gloss: I go in, come in, enter

verses.greek: καὶ ἐὰν μὲν ᾖ ἡ οἰκία ἀξία, ἐλθάτω ἡ εἰρήνη ὑμῶν ἐπ’ αὐτήν· ἐὰν δὲ μὴ ᾖ ἀξία, ἡ εἰρήνη ὑμῶν ⸀πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἐπιστραφήτω.
verses.mistral: And if the house is worthy, let peace from you be upon it: but if not, let your peace return to you.
verses.kjv: And if the house be worthy, let your peace come upon it: but if it be not worthy, let your peace return to you.

but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ ὃς ⸀ἂν μὴ δέξηται ὑμᾶς μηδὲ ἀκούσῃ τοὺς λόγους ὑμῶν, ἐξερχόμενοι ⸀ἔξω τῆς οἰκίας ἢ τῆς πόλεως ἐκείνης ἐκτινάξατε τὸν κονιορτὸν τῶν ποδῶν ὑμῶν.
verses.mistral: And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear your words, as ye go forth out of the house or city, shake off the dust from your feet.
verses.kjv: And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear your words, when ye depart out of that house or city, shake off the dust of your feet.

words.gloss: an untranslatable word that makes a statement contingent

verses.greek: ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν, ἀνεκτότερον ἔσται γῇ Σοδόμων καὶ Γομόρρων ἐν ἡμέρᾳ κρίσεως ἢ τῇ πόλει ἐκείνῃ.
verses.mistral: The kingdom of Sodom and Gomorrah will be better off on the day of judgment than that city.
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of judgment, than for that city.

words.gloss: verily, truly, amen, so let it be

verses.greek: Ἰδοὺ ἐγὼ ἀποστέλλω ὑμᾶς ὡς πρόβατα ἐν μέσῳ λύκων· γίνεσθε οὖν φρόνιμοι ὡς οἱ ὄφεις καὶ ἀκέραιοι ὡς αἱ περιστεραί.
verses.mistral: I send you out as sheep in the midst of wolves. Therefore be wise as serpents and innocent as doves.
verses.kjv: ¶ Behold, I send you forth as sheep in the midst of wolves: be ye therefore wise as serpents, and harmless as doves.

I come into being, am born

verses.mistral: They will deliver you up to councils, and in their synagogues they will scourge you.
verses.kjv: But beware of men: for they will deliver you up to the councils, and they will scourge you in their synagogues;

verses.mistral: And before governors and kings you will be brought because of me as a witness to them and to the Gentiles.
verses.kjv: And ye shall be brought before governors and kings for my sake, for a testimony against them and the Gentiles.

verses.greek: ὅταν δὲ ⸀παραδῶσιν ὑμᾶς, μὴ μεριμνήσητε πῶς ἢ τί λαλήσητε· δοθήσεται γὰρ ὑμῖν ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ὥρᾳ τί ⸀λαλήσητε·
verses.mistral: When they hand you over, do not worry about how or what you are to say. For in that hour, what you are to say will be given to you.
verses.kjv: But when they deliver you up, take no thought how or what ye shall speak: for it shall be given you in that same hour what ye shall speak.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.mistral: The Spirit of your Father is speaking through you.
verses.kjv: For it is not ye that speak, but the Spirit of your Father which speaketh in you.

verses.greek: παραδώσει δὲ ἀδελφὸς ἀδελφὸν εἰς θάνατον καὶ πατὴρ τέκνον, καὶ ἐπαναστήσονται τέκνα ἐπὶ γονεῖς καὶ θανατώσουσιν αὐτούς.
verses.mistral: Brothers will betray brothers to death, and fathers their children; and children will rise against parents and murder them.
verses.kjv: And the brother shall deliver up the brother to death, and the father the child: and the children shall rise up against their parents, and cause them to be put to death.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.mistral: And you will be hated by all because of my name. But he who endures to the end will be saved.
verses.kjv: And ye shall be hated of all men for my name's sake: but he that endureth to the end shall be saved.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὅταν δὲ διώκωσιν ὑμᾶς ἐν τῇ πόλει ταύτῃ, φεύγετε εἰς τὴν ⸀ἑτέραν· ἀμὴν γὰρ λέγω ὑμῖν, οὐ μὴ τελέσητε τὰς πόλεις ⸀τοῦ Ἰσραὴλ ἕως ⸀ἂν ἔλθῃ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου.
verses.mistral: Flee to the other city when they persecute you in this city, for truly I tell you, you will not have completed the cities of Israel until the Son of Man comes.
verses.kjv: But when they persecute you in this city, flee ye into another: for verily I say unto you, Ye shall not have gone over the cities of Israel, till the Son of man be come.

verses.mistral: Not a servant is greater than his master, nor is a disciple greater than his teacher.
verses.kjv: The disciple is not above his master, nor the servant above his lord.

verses.greek: ἀρκετὸν τῷ μαθητῇ ἵνα γένηται ὡς ὁ διδάσκαλος αὐτοῦ, καὶ ὁ δοῦλος ὡς ὁ κύριος αὐτοῦ. εἰ τὸν οἰκοδεσπότην Βεελζεβοὺλ ⸀ἐπεκάλεσαν, πόσῳ μᾶλλον τοὺς οἰκιακοὺς αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Enough for the disciple to become as his teacher, and the servant as his lord. If they called the Master of Beelzebul, how much more his household.
verses.kjv: It is enough for the disciple that he be as his master, and the servant as his lord. If they have called the master of the house Beelzebub, how much more shall they call them of his household?

I call by an additional name, appeal to

verses.greek: Μὴ οὖν φοβηθῆτε αὐτούς· οὐδὲν γάρ ἐστιν κεκαλυμμένον ὃ οὐκ ἀποκαλυφθήσεται, καὶ κρυπτὸν ὃ οὐ γνωσθήσεται.
verses.mistral: Fear them not; nothing is covered that shall not be revealed, and hidden that shall not be known.
verses.kjv: Fear them not therefore: for there is nothing covered, that shall not be revealed; and hid, that shall not be known.

words.gloss: I uncover, bring to light, reveal
I come to know, learn, realize

verses.greek: ὃ λέγω ὑμῖν ἐν τῇ σκοτίᾳ, εἴπατε ἐν τῷ φωτί· καὶ ὃ εἰς τὸ οὖς ἀκούετε, κηρύξατε ἐπὶ τῶν δωμάτων.
verses.mistral: Speak in the light what you hear in darkness; and what you have heard in your ear, declare on the housetops.
verses.kjv: What I tell you in darkness, that speak ye in light: and what ye hear in the ear, that preach ye upon the housetops.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.mistral: Fear not those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul; rather fear him who can destroy both soul and body in hell.
verses.kjv: And fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul: but rather fear him which is able to destroy both soul and body in hell.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: οὐχὶ δύο στρουθία ἀσσαρίου πωλεῖται; καὶ ἓν ἐξ αὐτῶν οὐ πεσεῖται ἐπὶ τὴν γῆν ἄνευ τοῦ πατρὸς ὑμῶν.
verses.mistral: Two sparrows are not sold for a penny. And not one of them will fall to the ground apart from your Father.
verses.kjv: Are not two sparrows sold for a farthing? and one of them shall not fall on the ground without your Father.

words.gloss: by no means, not at all
on, to, against, on the basis of, at
without, without the cooperation or knowledge of

verses.greek: ὑμῶν δὲ καὶ αἱ τρίχες τῆς κεφαλῆς πᾶσαι ἠριθμημέναι εἰσίν.
verses.mistral: Your hairs are all numbered.
verses.kjv: But the very hairs of your head are all numbered.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: μὴ οὖν ⸀φοβεῖσθε· πολλῶν στρουθίων διαφέρετε ὑμεῖς.
verses.mistral: Fear not, ye are of more value than many sparrows.
verses.kjv: Fear ye not therefore, ye are of more value than many sparrows.

words.gloss: I carry through, differ, surpass, excel

verses.greek: Πᾶς οὖν ὅστις ὁμολογήσει ἐν ἐμοὶ ἔμπροσθεν τῶν ἀνθρώπων, ὁμολογήσω κἀγὼ ἐν αὐτῷ ἔμπροσθεν τοῦ πατρός μου τοῦ ⸀ἐν οὐρανοῖς·
verses.mistral: Confesses in me before men, I also will confess in him before my Father who is in heaven.
verses.kjv: Whosoever therefore shall confess me before men, him will I confess also before my Father which is in heaven.

verses.greek: ὅστις ⸂δ’ ἂν⸃ ἀρνήσηταί με ἔμπροσθεν τῶν ἀνθρώπων, ἀρνήσομαι ⸂κἀγὼ αὐτὸν⸃ ἔμπροσθεν τοῦ πατρός μου τοῦ ⸀ἐν οὐρανοῖς.
verses.mistral: Whoever denies me before men, I also will deny him before my Father who is in heaven.
verses.kjv: But whosoever shall deny me before men, him will I also deny before my Father which is in heaven.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Μὴ νομίσητε ὅτι ἦλθον βαλεῖν εἰρήνην ἐπὶ τὴν γῆν· οὐκ ἦλθον βαλεῖν εἰρήνην ἀλλὰ μάχαιραν.
verses.mistral: I did not come to bring peace, but a sword.
verses.kjv: Think not that I am come to send peace on earth: I came not to send peace, but a sword.

on, to, against, on the basis of, at
I cast, throw, rush, put, place, drop

verses.greek: ἦλθον γὰρ διχάσαι ἄνθρωπον κατὰ τοῦ πατρὸς αὐτοῦ καὶ θυγατέρα κατὰ τῆς μητρὸς αὐτῆς καὶ νύμφην κατὰ τῆς πενθερᾶς αὐτῆς,
verses.mistral: I have come to set a man against his father, and a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.
verses.kjv: For I am come to set a man at variance against his father, and the daughter against her mother, and the daughter in law against her mother in law.

words.gloss: I set at variance, make to be hostile
down from, against, according to, throughout, during
down from, against, according to, throughout, during
