verses.greek: καὶ ἐχθροὶ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου οἱ οἰκιακοὶ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Enemies of the man are his household members.
verses.kjv: And a man's foes shall be they of his own household.

verses.greek: ὁ φιλῶν πατέρα ἢ μητέρα ὑπὲρ ἐμὲ οὐκ ἔστιν μου ἄξιος· καὶ ὁ φιλῶν υἱὸν ἢ θυγατέρα ὑπὲρ ἐμὲ οὐκ ἔστιν μου ἄξιος·
verses.mistral: Loving father or mother, or son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me
verses.kjv: He that loveth father or mother more than me is not worthy of me: and he that loveth son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me.

verses.greek: καὶ ὃς οὐ λαμβάνει τὸν σταυρὸν αὐτοῦ καὶ ἀκολουθεῖ ὀπίσω μου, οὐκ ἔστιν μου ἄξιος.
verses.mistral: And he who does not take up his cross and follow me is not worthy of me.
verses.kjv: And he that taketh not his cross, and followeth after me, is not worthy of me.

verses.greek: ὁ εὑρὼν τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ ἀπολέσει αὐτήν, καὶ ὁ ἀπολέσας τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ ἕνεκεν ἐμοῦ εὑρήσει αὐτήν.
verses.mistral: Whoever finds his life will lose it, and whoever loses his life for my sake will find it.
verses.kjv: He that findeth his life shall lose it: and he that loseth his life for my sake shall find it.

words.gloss: for the sake of, on account of, wherefore

verses.greek: Ὁ δεχόμενος ὑμᾶς ἐμὲ δέχεται, καὶ ὁ ἐμὲ δεχόμενος δέχεται τὸν ἀποστείλαντά με.
verses.mistral: The one receiving you receives me, and the one receiving me receives the one who sent me.
verses.kjv: ¶ He that receiveth you receiveth me, and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.

words.gloss: I send forth, send, send away, dismiss

verses.greek: ὁ δεχόμενος προφήτην εἰς ὄνομα προφήτου μισθὸν προφήτου λήμψεται, καὶ ὁ δεχόμενος δίκαιον εἰς ὄνομα δικαίου μισθὸν δικαίου λήμψεται.
verses.mistral: The one receiving a prophet in the name of a prophet shall receive the reward of a prophet, and the one receiving a righteous person in the name of a righteous person shall receive the reward of the righteous.
verses.kjv: He that receiveth a prophet in the name of a prophet shall receive a prophet's reward; and he that receiveth a righteous man in the name of a righteous man shall receive a righteous man's reward.

verses.greek: καὶ ὃς ⸀ἂν ποτίσῃ ἕνα τῶν μικρῶν τούτων ποτήριον ψυχροῦ μόνον εἰς ὄνομα μαθητοῦ, ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν, οὐ μὴ ἀπολέσῃ τὸν μισθὸν αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: And whoever gives even a cup of cold water to one of these little ones in the name of a disciple, truly I say to you, he will not lose his reward.
verses.kjv: And whosoever shall give to drink unto one of these little ones a cup of cold water only in the name of a disciple, verily I say unto you, he shall in no wise lose his reward.

words.gloss: an untranslatable word that makes a statement contingent
I cause to drink, give to drink
verily, truly, amen, so let it be

verses.greek: Καὶ ἐγένετο ὅτε ἐτέλεσεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς διατάσσων τοῖς δώδεκα μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ, μετέβη ἐκεῖθεν τοῦ διδάσκειν καὶ κηρύσσειν ἐν ταῖς πόλεσιν αὐτῶν.
verses.mistral: And it came to pass, when Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he departed from there to teach and preach in their cities.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when Jesus had made an end of commanding his twelve disciples, he departed thence to teach and to preach in their cities.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born
I leave, depart, remove, pass over

verses.greek: Ὁ δὲ Ἰωάννης ἀκούσας ἐν τῷ δεσμωτηρίῳ τὰ ἔργα τοῦ χριστοῦ πέμψας ⸀διὰ τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ
verses.mistral: John, hearing in prison the works of Christ, sent his disciples
verses.kjv: Now when John had heard in the prison the works of Christ, he sent two of his disciples,

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: εἶπεν αὐτῷ· Σὺ εἶ ὁ ἐρχόμενος ἢ ἕτερον προσδοκῶμεν;
verses.mistral: You are the one who is coming, or do we expect another?
verses.kjv: And said unto him, Art thou he that should come, or do we look for another?

verses.greek: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Πορευθέντες ἀπαγγείλατε Ἰωάννῃ ἃ ἀκούετε καὶ βλέπετε·
verses.mistral: Announce to John what you hear and see.
verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto them, Go and shew John again those things which ye do hear and see:

words.gloss: I report, bring a report, announce

verses.greek: τυφλοὶ ἀναβλέπουσιν καὶ χωλοὶ περιπατοῦσιν, λεπροὶ καθαρίζονται καὶ κωφοὶ ἀκούουσιν, ⸀καὶ νεκροὶ ἐγείρονται καὶ πτωχοὶ εὐαγγελίζονται·
verses.mistral: Blind receive sight, lame walk, lepers are cleansed, deaf hear, dead are raised, and poor are preached to.
verses.kjv: The blind receive their sight, and the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, and the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the poor have the gospel preached to them.

words.gloss: I look up, recover my sight
I bring good news, preach good tidings

verses.greek: καὶ μακάριός ἐστιν ὃς ⸀ἐὰν μὴ σκανδαλισθῇ ἐν ἐμοί.
verses.mistral: Blessed is he who does not cause me to stumble.
verses.kjv: And blessed is he, whosoever shall not be offended in me.

verses.greek: Τούτων δὲ πορευομένων ἤρξατο ὁ Ἰησοῦς λέγειν τοῖς ὄχλοις περὶ Ἰωάννου· Τί ἐξήλθατε εἰς τὴν ἔρημον θεάσασθαι; κάλαμον ὑπὸ ἀνέμου σαλευόμενον;
verses.mistral: What went out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?
verses.kjv: ¶ And as they departed, Jesus began to say unto the multitudes concerning John, What went ye out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken with the wind?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ τί ἐξήλθατε ἰδεῖν; ἄνθρωπον ἐν ⸀μαλακοῖς ἠμφιεσμένον; ἰδοὺ οἱ τὰ μαλακὰ φοροῦντες ἐν τοῖς οἴκοις τῶν ⸀βασιλέων ⸀εἰσίν.
verses.mistral: What went out to see? A man clothed in soft garments? Behold, those who wear soft clothes are in the houses of kings.
verses.kjv: But what went ye out for to see? A man clothed in soft raiment? behold, they that wear soft clothing are in kings' houses.

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ τί ἐξήλθατε⸂; προφήτην ἰδεῖν⸃; ναί, λέγω ὑμῖν, καὶ περισσότερον προφήτου.
verses.mistral: What went out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
verses.kjv: But what went ye out for to see? A prophet? yea, I say unto you, and more than a prophet.

verses.greek: ⸀οὗτός ἐστιν περὶ οὗ γέγραπται· Ἰδοὺ ἐγὼ ἀποστέλλω τὸν ἄγγελόν μου πρὸ προσώπου σου, ὃς κατασκευάσει τὴν ὁδόν σου ἔμπροσθέν σου.
verses.mistral: This is about the one who is written about: "Indeed, I am sending My angel before you, who will prepare your way before you."
verses.kjv: For this is he, of whom it is written, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.

words.gloss: I send forth, send, send away, dismiss

verses.greek: ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν, οὐκ ἐγήγερται ἐν γεννητοῖς γυναικῶν μείζων Ἰωάννου τοῦ βαπτιστοῦ· ὁ δὲ μικρότερος ἐν τῇ βασιλείᾳ τῶν οὐρανῶν μείζων αὐτοῦ ἐστιν.
verses.mistral: Assuredly, I say to you, no one born of women is greater than John the Baptist; but he who is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, Among them that are born of women there hath not risen a greater than John the Baptist: notwithstanding he that is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.

words.gloss: verily, truly, amen, so let it be
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἀπὸ δὲ τῶν ἡμερῶν Ἰωάννου τοῦ βαπτιστοῦ ἕως ἄρτι ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν βιάζεται, καὶ βιασταὶ ἁρπάζουσιν αὐτήν.
verses.mistral: From the days of John the Baptist until now, the kingdom of heaven suffers violence, and violent men seize it.
verses.kjv: And from the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven suffereth violence, and the violent take it by force.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I seize, snatch, obtain by robbery

verses.greek: πάντες γὰρ οἱ προφῆται καὶ ὁ νόμος ἕως Ἰωάννου ἐπροφήτευσαν·
verses.mistral: All prophets and the law prophesied until John.
verses.kjv: For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John.

verses.greek: καὶ εἰ θέλετε δέξασθαι, αὐτός ἐστιν Ἠλίας ὁ μέλλων ἔρχεσθαι.
verses.mistral: And if you will accept it, he is Elias (Elijah) who is coming.
verses.kjv: And if ye will receive it, this is Elias, which was for to come.

verses.greek: ὁ ἔχων ⸀ὦτα ἀκουέτω.
verses.mistral: Listening one.
verses.kjv: He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

verses.greek: Τίνι δὲ ὁμοιώσω τὴν γενεὰν ταύτην; ὁμοία ἐστὶν παιδίοις ⸂καθημένοις ἐν ταῖς ἀγοραῖς ἃ προσφωνοῦντα τοῖς ἑτέροις⸃
verses.mistral: This generation is like children crying out to others in the marketplaces.
verses.kjv: ¶ But whereunto shall I liken this generation? It is like unto children sitting in the markets, and calling unto their fellows,

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ⸀λέγουσιν· Ηὐλήσαμεν ὑμῖν καὶ οὐκ ὠρχήσασθε· ⸀ἐθρηνήσαμεν καὶ οὐκ ἐκόψασθε·
verses.mistral: Mourned, but you did not weep.
verses.kjv: And saying, We have piped unto you, and ye have not danced; we have mourned unto you, and ye have not lamented.

verses.greek: ἦλθεν γὰρ Ἰωάννης μήτε ἐσθίων μήτε πίνων, καὶ λέγουσιν· Δαιμόνιον ἔχει·
verses.mistral: Fasted, for John ate nothing and drank nothing, and they were saying, "He has a demon."
verses.kjv: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He hath a devil.

verses.greek: ἦλθεν ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐσθίων καὶ πίνων, καὶ λέγουσιν· Ἰδοὺ ἄνθρωπος φάγος καὶ οἰνοπότης, τελωνῶν φίλος καὶ ἁμαρτωλῶν. καὶ ἐδικαιώθη ἡ σοφία ἀπὸ τῶν ⸀ἔργων αὐτῆς.
verses.mistral: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they said, "Behold, a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners." And wisdom was justified by her deeds.
verses.kjv: The Son of man came eating and drinking, and they say, Behold a man gluttonous, and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners. But wisdom is justified of her children.

words.gloss: I make righteous, defend the cause of, justify

verses.greek: Τότε ⸀ἤρξατο ὀνειδίζειν τὰς πόλεις ἐν αἷς ἐγένοντο αἱ πλεῖσται δυνάμεις αὐτοῦ, ὅτι οὐ μετενόησαν·
verses.mistral: Then he began to denounce the cities in which most of his mighty works had been done, because they did not repent.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then began he to upbraid the cities wherein most of his mighty works were done, because they repented not:

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: Οὐαί σοι, Χοραζίν· οὐαί σοι, Βηθσαϊδά· ὅτι εἰ ἐν Τύρῳ καὶ Σιδῶνι ἐγένοντο αἱ δυνάμεις αἱ γενόμεναι ἐν ὑμῖν, πάλαι ἂν ἐν σάκκῳ καὶ σποδῷ μετενόησαν.
verses.mistral: Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! If the mighty works that were done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
verses.kjv: Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works, which were done in you, had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born
I come into being, am born
an untranslatable word that makes a statement contingent

verses.greek: πλὴν λέγω ὑμῖν, Τύρῳ καὶ Σιδῶνι ἀνεκτότερον ἔσται ἐν ἡμέρᾳ κρίσεως ἢ ὑμῖν.
verses.mistral: In the day of judgment, Tyre and Sidon will be superior to you.
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the day of judgment, than for you.

verses.greek: καὶ σύ, Καφαρναούμ, ⸂μὴ ἕως οὐρανοῦ ὑψωθήσῃ⸃; ἕως ᾅδου ⸀καταβήσῃ· ὅτι εἰ ἐν Σοδόμοις ⸀ἐγενήθησαν αἱ δυνάμεις αἱ γενόμεναι ἐν σοί, ⸀ἔμεινεν ἂν μέχρι τῆς σήμερον.
verses.mistral: And you, Capernaum, will you not be exalted to heaven? You shall be brought down to Hades; for if in Sodom such things as are done in you had been done, it would have remained until this day.
verses.kjv: And thou, Capernaum, which art exalted unto heaven, shalt be brought down to hell: for if the mighty works, which have been done in thee, had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until this day.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born
I come into being, am born
an untranslatable word that makes a statement contingent

verses.greek: πλὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι γῇ Σοδόμων ἀνεκτότερον ἔσται ἐν ἡμέρᾳ κρίσεως ἢ σοί.
verses.mistral: In the day of judgment, Sodom will be superior to you.
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That it shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom in the day of judgment, than for thee.

verses.greek: Ἐν ἐκείνῳ τῷ καιρῷ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν· Ἐξομολογοῦμαί σοι, πάτερ κύριε τοῦ οὐρανοῦ καὶ τῆς γῆς, ὅτι ⸀ἔκρυψας ταῦτα ἀπὸ σοφῶν καὶ συνετῶν, καὶ ἀπεκάλυψας αὐτὰ νηπίοις·
verses.mistral: In that time responding, Jesus said: I confess to You, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that You have hidden these things from wise and intelligent people, and have revealed them to little children.
verses.kjv: ¶ At that time Jesus answered and said, I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes.

words.gloss: I uncover, bring to light, reveal

verses.greek: ναί, ὁ πατήρ, ὅτι οὕτως ⸂εὐδοκία ἐγένετο⸃ ἔμπροσθέν σου.
verses.mistral: Indeed, Father, for thus Your will has been done.
verses.kjv: Even so, Father: for so it seemed good in thy sight.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: Πάντα μοι παρεδόθη ὑπὸ τοῦ πατρός μου, καὶ οὐδεὶς ἐπιγινώσκει τὸν υἱὸν εἰ μὴ ὁ πατήρ, οὐδὲ τὸν πατέρα τις ἐπιγινώσκει εἰ μὴ ὁ υἱὸς καὶ ᾧ ἐὰν βούληται ὁ υἱὸς ἀποκαλύψαι.
verses.mistral: All things have been handed over to me by my Father, and no one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son, and whoever the Son wills to reveal him.
verses.kjv: All things are delivered unto me of my Father: and no man knoweth the Son, but the Father; neither knoweth any man the Father, save the Son, and he to whomsoever the Son will reveal him.

words.gloss: I come to know, discern, recognize
I come to know, discern, recognize
I uncover, bring to light, reveal

verses.greek: Δεῦτε πρός με πάντες οἱ κοπιῶντες καὶ πεφορτισμένοι, κἀγὼ ἀναπαύσω ὑμᾶς.
verses.mistral: Come to me all who are heavy-laden and I will give you rest.
verses.kjv: ¶ Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.

words.gloss: I make to rest, give rest to, rest, take my ease

verses.greek: ἄρατε τὸν ζυγόν μου ἐφ’ ὑμᾶς καὶ μάθετε ἀπ’ ἐμοῦ, ὅτι πραΰς εἰμι καὶ ταπεινὸς τῇ καρδίᾳ, καὶ εὑρήσετε ἀνάπαυσιν ταῖς ψυχαῖς ὑμῶν·
verses.mistral: Carry my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and lowly in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.
verses.kjv: Take my yoke upon you, and learn of me; for I am meek and lowly in heart: and ye shall find rest unto your souls.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: ὁ γὰρ ζυγός μου χρηστὸς καὶ τὸ φορτίον μου ἐλαφρόν ἐστιν.
verses.mistral: My yoke is easy, and my burden is light.
verses.kjv: For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.

verses.greek: Ἐν ἐκείνῳ τῷ καιρῷ ἐπορεύθη ὁ Ἰησοῦς τοῖς σάββασιν διὰ τῶν σπορίμων· οἱ δὲ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ ἐπείνασαν καὶ ἤρξαντο τίλλειν στάχυας καὶ ἐσθίειν.
verses.mistral: In that very time, Jesus went on the Sabbath day through the fields; his disciples were hungry and began to pick heads of grain and eat them.
verses.kjv: At that time Jesus went on the sabbath day through the corn; and his disciples were an hungred, and began to pluck the ears of corn, and to eat.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ Φαρισαῖοι ἰδόντες εἶπαν αὐτῷ· Ἰδοὺ οἱ μαθηταί σου ποιοῦσιν ὃ οὐκ ἔξεστιν ποιεῖν ἐν σαββάτῳ.
verses.mistral: The Pharisees, seeing this, said to Him, "Behold, Your disciples are doing what is not lawful to do on the Sabbath."
verses.kjv: But when the Pharisees saw it, they said unto him, Behold, thy disciples do that which is not lawful to do upon the sabbath day.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Οὐκ ἀνέγνωτε τί ἐποίησεν Δαυὶδ ὅτε ⸀ἐπείνασεν καὶ οἱ μετ’ αὐτοῦ;
verses.mistral: He said to them, "Have you not read what David did when he was hungry and those with him?"
verses.kjv: But he said unto them, Have ye not read what David did, when he was an hungred, and they that were with him;

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: πῶς εἰσῆλθεν εἰς τὸν οἶκον τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ τοὺς ἄρτους τῆς προθέσεως ⸂ἔφαγον, ὃ⸃ οὐκ ἐξὸν ἦν αὐτῷ φαγεῖν οὐδὲ τοῖς μετ’ αὐτοῦ, εἰ μὴ τοῖς ἱερεῦσιν μόνοις;
verses.mistral: How entered he into the house of God and did eat the loaves of the offering, which were not lawful for him nor for those with him, except for the priests only?
verses.kjv: How he entered into the house of God, and did eat the shewbread, which was not lawful for him to eat, neither for them which were with him, but only for the priests?

words.gloss: I go in, come in, enter

verses.greek: ἢ οὐκ ἀνέγνωτε ἐν τῷ νόμῳ ὅτι τοῖς σάββασιν οἱ ἱερεῖς ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ τὸ σάββατον βεβηλοῦσιν καὶ ἀναίτιοί εἰσιν;
verses.mistral: Did you not read in the law that on Sabbath days, the priests in the temple profane it and are guiltless?
verses.kjv: Or have ye not read in the law, how that on the sabbath days the priests in the temple profane the sabbath, and are blameless?

verses.greek: λέγω δὲ ὑμῖν ὅτι τοῦ ἱεροῦ μεῖζόν ἐστιν ὧδε.
verses.mistral: The temple is greater here.
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That in this place is one greater than the temple.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: εἰ δὲ ἐγνώκειτε τί ἐστιν· Ἔλεος θέλω καὶ οὐ θυσίαν, οὐκ ἂν κατεδικάσατε τοὺς ἀναιτίους.
verses.mistral: Mercy I desire, and not sacrifice; had you known this, you would not have condemned the guiltless.
verses.kjv: But if ye had known what this meaneth, I will have mercy, and not sacrifice, ye would not have condemned the guiltless.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come to know, learn, realize
an untranslatable word that makes a statement contingent

verses.greek: κύριος γάρ ἐστιν τοῦ σαββάτου ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου.
verses.mistral: The Lord of Sabbath is the Son of Man.
verses.kjv: For the Son of man is Lord even of the sabbath day.

verses.greek: Καὶ μεταβὰς ἐκεῖθεν ἦλθεν εἰς τὴν συναγωγὴν αὐτῶν·
verses.mistral: Entering from there, he went into their synagogue
verses.kjv: And when he was departed thence, he went into their synagogue:

words.gloss: I leave, depart, remove, pass over

verses.greek: καὶ ἰδοὺ ⸀ἄνθρωπος χεῖρα ἔχων ξηράν. καὶ ἐπηρώτησαν αὐτὸν λέγοντες· Εἰ ἔξεστι τοῖς σάββασιν ⸀θεραπεύειν; ἵνα κατηγορήσωσιν αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: A man with a withered hand. And they asked him, saying: "Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?" that they might accuse him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And, behold, there was a man which had his hand withered. And they asked him, saying, Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath days? that they might accuse him.

words.gloss: I care for, attend, serve, heal

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Τίς ⸀ἔσται ἐξ ὑμῶν ἄνθρωπος ὃς ἕξει πρόβατον ἕν, καὶ ἐὰν ἐμπέσῃ τοῦτο τοῖς σάββασιν εἰς βόθυνον, οὐχὶ κρατήσει αὐτὸ καὶ ἐγερεῖ;
verses.mistral: He replied to them, "Which of you will have a sheep, and if it falls into a pit on the Sabbath day, will he not take hold of it and lift it out?"
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, What man shall there be among you, that shall have one sheep, and if it fall into a pit on the sabbath day, will he not lay hold on it, and lift it out?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I fall in, am cast in
by no means, not at all

verses.greek: πόσῳ οὖν διαφέρει ἄνθρωπος προβάτου. ὥστε ἔξεστιν τοῖς σάββασιν καλῶς ποιεῖν.
verses.mistral: Man is worth more than a sheep, therefore it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath.
verses.kjv: How much then is a man better than a sheep? Wherefore it is lawful to do well on the sabbath days.

words.gloss: I carry through, differ, surpass, excel

verses.greek: τότε λέγει τῷ ἀνθρώπῳ· Ἔκτεινόν ⸂σου τὴν χεῖρα⸃· καὶ ἐξέτεινεν, καὶ ἀπεκατεστάθη ὑγιὴς ὡς ἡ ἄλλη.
verses.mistral: Then he says to the man, "Stretch out your hand." And he stretched it out, and it was restored whole like the other.
verses.kjv: Then saith he to the man, Stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched it forth; and it was restored whole, like as the other.

verses.greek: ⸂ἐξελθόντες δὲ οἱ Φαρισαῖοι συμβούλιον ἔλαβον κατ’ αὐτοῦ⸃ ὅπως αὐτὸν ἀπολέσωσιν.
verses.mistral: They took a council against Him to destroy Him.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then the Pharisees went out, and held a council against him, how they might destroy him.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: Ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς γνοὺς ἀνεχώρησεν ἐκεῖθεν. καὶ ἠκολούθησαν ⸀αὐτῷ πολλοί, καὶ ἐθεράπευσεν αὐτοὺς πάντας,
verses.mistral: Jesus, knowing that, departed from there. Many followed Him, and He healed all of them.
verses.kjv: But when Jesus knew it, he withdrew himself from thence: and great multitudes followed him, and he healed them all;

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come to know, learn, realize
I care for, attend, serve, heal

verses.greek: καὶ ἐπετίμησεν αὐτοῖς ἵνα μὴ φανερὸν αὐτὸν ποιήσωσιν,
verses.mistral: Command them that it be not made manifest
verses.kjv: And charged them that they should not make him known:

verses.greek: ⸀ἵνα πληρωθῇ τὸ ῥηθὲν διὰ Ἠσαΐου τοῦ προφήτου λέγοντος·
verses.mistral: Fulfilled is that which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying:
verses.kjv: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Esaias the prophet, saying,

verses.greek: Ἰδοὺ ὁ παῖς μου ὃν ᾑρέτισα, ὁ ἀγαπητός μου ⸂εἰς ὃν⸃ εὐδόκησεν ἡ ψυχή μου· θήσω τὸ πνεῦμά μου ἐπ’ αὐτόν, καὶ κρίσιν τοῖς ἔθνεσιν ἀπαγγελεῖ.
verses.mistral: Behold My servant whom I have chosen, My beloved in whom My soul is well-pleased. I will put My Spirit upon Him, and He will declare justice to the nations.
verses.kjv: Behold my servant, whom I have chosen; my beloved, in whom my soul is well pleased: I will put my spirit upon him, and he shall shew judgment to the Gentiles.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at
I report, bring a report, announce

verses.greek: οὐκ ἐρίσει οὐδὲ κραυγάσει, οὐδὲ ἀκούσει τις ἐν ταῖς πλατείαις τὴν φωνὴν αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: He will not cry out or shout, nor will anyone hear his voice in the streets.
verses.kjv: He shall not strive, nor cry; neither shall any man hear his voice in the streets.

verses.greek: κάλαμον συντετριμμένον οὐ κατεάξει καὶ λίνον τυφόμενον οὐ σβέσει, ἕως ἂν ἐκβάλῃ εἰς νῖκος τὴν κρίσιν.
verses.mistral: Does not a reed shaken by the wind stand firm? Neither will this one fall by himself. He will be thrust into a furnace, and if he remains firm, then he will be glorified in the trial. [Matthew 12:20-21]
verses.kjv: A bruised reed shall he not break, and smoking flax shall he not quench, till he send forth judgment unto victory.

words.gloss: an untranslatable word that makes a statement contingent
I throw, cast, put out, banish, bring forth, produce

verses.greek: καὶ τῷ ὀνόματι αὐτοῦ ἔθνη ἐλπιοῦσιν.
verses.mistral: And nations hope in his name.
verses.kjv: And in his name shall the Gentiles trust.

verses.greek: Τότε ⸂προσηνέχθη αὐτῷ δαιμονιζόμενος τυφλὸς καὶ κωφός⸃· καὶ ἐθεράπευσεν αὐτόν, ὥστε τὸν ⸀κωφὸν λαλεῖν καὶ βλέπειν.
verses.mistral: Then a mute and blind man was brought to him. And he healed him, so that the mute could speak and see.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then was brought unto him one possessed with a devil, blind, and dumb: and he healed him, insomuch that the blind and dumb both spake and saw.

words.gloss: I care for, attend, serve, heal

verses.greek: καὶ ἐξίσταντο πάντες οἱ ὄχλοι καὶ ἔλεγον· Μήτι οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ υἱὸς Δαυίδ;
verses.mistral: This one is the Son of David?
verses.kjv: And all the people were amazed, and said, Is not this the son of David?

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ Φαρισαῖοι ἀκούσαντες εἶπον· Οὗτος οὐκ ἐκβάλλει τὰ δαιμόνια εἰ μὴ ἐν τῷ Βεελζεβοὺλ ἄρχοντι τῶν δαιμονίων.
verses.mistral: The Pharisees hearing said, "This one does not cast out demons except by Beelzebub, the chief of demons."
verses.kjv: But when the Pharisees heard it, they said, This fellow doth not cast out devils, but by Beelzebub the prince of the devils.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I throw, cast, put out, banish, bring forth, produce

verses.greek: εἰδὼς ⸀δὲ τὰς ἐνθυμήσεις αὐτῶν εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Πᾶσα βασιλεία μερισθεῖσα καθ’ ἑαυτῆς ἐρημοῦται, καὶ πᾶσα πόλις ἢ οἰκία μερισθεῖσα καθ’ ἑαυτῆς οὐ σταθήσεται.
verses.mistral: All divided kingdoms will be emptied, and every city or house divided against itself will not stand.
verses.kjv: And Jesus knew their thoughts, and said unto them, Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and every city or house divided against itself shall not stand:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
down from, against, according to, throughout, during
down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: καὶ εἰ ὁ Σατανᾶς τὸν Σατανᾶν ἐκβάλλει, ἐφ’ ἑαυτὸν ἐμερίσθη· πῶς οὖν σταθήσεται ἡ βασιλεία αὐτοῦ;
verses.mistral: If Satan casts out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then will his kingdom stand?
verses.kjv: And if Satan cast out Satan, he is divided against himself; how shall then his kingdom stand?

words.gloss: I throw, cast, put out, banish, bring forth, produce
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: καὶ εἰ ἐγὼ ἐν Βεελζεβοὺλ ἐκβάλλω τὰ δαιμόνια, οἱ υἱοὶ ὑμῶν ἐν τίνι ἐκβάλλουσιν; διὰ τοῦτο αὐτοὶ ⸂κριταὶ ἔσονται ὑμῶν⸃.
verses.mistral: And if I by Beelzebul cast out demons, by whom do your sons cast them out? Therefore they shall be your judges.
verses.kjv: And if I by Beelzebub cast out devils, by whom do your children cast them out? therefore they shall be your judges.

words.gloss: I throw, cast, put out, banish, bring forth, produce
I throw, cast, put out, banish, bring forth, produce

verses.greek: εἰ δὲ ἐν πνεύματι θεοῦ ἐγὼ ἐκβάλλω τὰ δαιμόνια, ἄρα ἔφθασεν ἐφ’ ὑμᾶς ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: If I cast out demons by the Spirit of God, then has come among you the kingdom of God.
verses.kjv: But if I cast out devils by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God is come unto you.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I throw, cast, put out, banish, bring forth, produce
a particle asking a question, expecting a negative response
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: ἢ πῶς δύναταί τις εἰσελθεῖν εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν τοῦ ἰσχυροῦ καὶ τὰ σκεύη αὐτοῦ ⸀ἁρπάσαι, ἐὰν μὴ πρῶτον δήσῃ τὸν ἰσχυρόν; καὶ τότε τὴν οἰκίαν αὐτοῦ ⸀διαρπάσει.
verses.mistral: Can a strong man, when he is fully armed, be robbed of his possessions, unless first he is bound? And then the house of the robber will be plundered.
verses.kjv: Or else how can one enter into a strong man's house, and spoil his goods, except he first bind the strong man? and then he will spoil his house.

words.gloss: I go in, come in, enter
I seize, snatch, obtain by robbery

verses.greek: ὁ μὴ ὢν μετ’ ἐμοῦ κατ’ ἐμοῦ ἐστιν, καὶ ὁ μὴ συνάγων μετ’ ἐμοῦ σκορπίζει.
verses.mistral: Not with me is he who does not gather with me. He scatters.
verses.kjv: He that is not with me is against me; and he that gathereth not with me scattereth abroad.

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: διὰ τοῦτο λέγω ὑμῖν, πᾶσα ἁμαρτία καὶ βλασφημία ἀφεθήσεται τοῖς ἀνθρώποις, ἡ δὲ τοῦ πνεύματος βλασφημία οὐκ ⸀ἀφεθήσεται.
verses.mistral: Therefore I tell you, every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven people, but the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven.
verses.kjv: ¶ Wherefore I say unto you, All manner of sin and blasphemy shall be forgiven unto men: but the blasphemy against the Holy Ghost shall not be forgiven unto men.

words.gloss: I send away, release, remit, forgive, permit
but, on the other hand, and
I send away, release, remit, forgive, permit

verses.greek: καὶ ὃς ἐὰν εἴπῃ λόγον κατὰ τοῦ υἱοῦ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου, ἀφεθήσεται αὐτῷ· ὃς δ’ ἂν εἴπῃ κατὰ τοῦ πνεύματος τοῦ ἁγίου, οὐκ ἀφεθήσεται αὐτῷ οὔτε ἐν ⸂τούτῳ τῷ⸃ αἰῶνι οὔτε ἐν τῷ μέλλοντι.
verses.mistral: And whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven in this age nor in the age to come.
verses.kjv: And whosoever speaketh a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him: but whosoever speaketh against the Holy Ghost, it shall not be forgiven him, neither in this world, neither in the world to come.

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during
I send away, release, remit, forgive, permit
but, on the other hand, and
an untranslatable word that makes a statement contingent
down from, against, according to, throughout, during
I send away, release, remit, forgive, permit

verses.greek: Ἢ ποιήσατε τὸ δένδρον καλὸν καὶ τὸν καρπὸν αὐτοῦ καλόν, ἢ ποιήσατε τὸ δένδρον σαπρὸν καὶ τὸν καρπὸν αὐτοῦ σαπρόν· ἐκ γὰρ τοῦ καρποῦ τὸ δένδρον γινώσκεται.
verses.mistral: The good tree produces good fruit, and the bad tree produces bad fruit. A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, nor can a bad tree produce good fruit. The tree is known by its fruit.
verses.kjv: Either make the tree good, and his fruit good; or else make the tree corrupt, and his fruit corrupt: for the tree is known by his fruit.

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize

verses.greek: γεννήματα ἐχιδνῶν, πῶς δύνασθε ἀγαθὰ λαλεῖν πονηροὶ ὄντες; ἐκ γὰρ τοῦ περισσεύματος τῆς καρδίας τὸ στόμα λαλεῖ.
verses.mistral: Speaking good things, how can evil men do? For out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaks.
verses.kjv: O generation of vipers, how can ye, being evil, speak good things? for out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh.

verses.greek: ὁ ἀγαθὸς ἄνθρωπος ἐκ τοῦ ἀγαθοῦ θησαυροῦ ἐκβάλλει ἀγαθά, καὶ ὁ πονηρὸς ἄνθρωπος ἐκ τοῦ πονηροῦ θησαυροῦ ἐκβάλλει πονηρά.
verses.mistral: The good man brings out of his good treasury what is good, and the evil man brings out of his evil treasury what is evil.
verses.kjv: A good man out of the good treasure of the heart bringeth forth good things: and an evil man out of the evil treasure bringeth forth evil things.

words.gloss: I throw, cast, put out, banish, bring forth, produce
I throw, cast, put out, banish, bring forth, produce

verses.greek: λέγω δὲ ὑμῖν ὅτι πᾶν ῥῆμα ἀργὸν ὃ ⸀λαλήσουσιν οἱ ἄνθρωποι, ἀποδώσουσιν περὶ αὐτοῦ λόγον ἐν ἡμέρᾳ κρίσεως·
verses.mistral: I tell you that on the day of judgment people will give an account for every careless word they speak.
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That every idle word that men shall speak, they shall give account thereof in the day of judgment.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἐκ γὰρ τῶν λόγων σου δικαιωθήσῃ, καὶ ἐκ τῶν λόγων σου καταδικασθήσῃ.
verses.mistral: From your words you will be justified, and by your words you will be condemned.
verses.kjv: For by thy words thou shalt be justified, and by thy words thou shalt be condemned.

words.gloss: I make righteous, defend the cause of, justify

verses.greek: Τότε ἀπεκρίθησαν ⸀αὐτῷ τινες τῶν γραμματέων καὶ Φαρισαίων λέγοντες· Διδάσκαλε, θέλομεν ἀπὸ σοῦ σημεῖον ἰδεῖν.
verses.mistral: They answered Him then some of the Pharisees and scribes, saying: "Teacher, we desire to see a sign from You."
verses.kjv: ¶ Then certain of the scribes and of the Pharisees answered, saying, Master, we would see a sign from thee.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Γενεὰ πονηρὰ καὶ μοιχαλὶς σημεῖον ἐπιζητεῖ, καὶ σημεῖον οὐ δοθήσεται αὐτῇ εἰ μὴ τὸ σημεῖον Ἰωνᾶ τοῦ προφήτου.
verses.mistral: Adulterous and sinful generation, it is asked for a sign from you, but it will not be given to you unless the sign of Jonah is given to you.
verses.kjv: But he answered and said unto them, An evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given to it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I seek after, desire, search for

verses.greek: ὥσπερ γὰρ ἦν Ἰωνᾶς ἐν τῇ κοιλίᾳ τοῦ κήτους τρεῖς ἡμέρας καὶ τρεῖς νύκτας, οὕτως ἔσται ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ τῆς γῆς τρεῖς ἡμέρας καὶ τρεῖς νύκτας.
verses.mistral: The Son of Man will be in the heart of the earth for three days and three nights, just as Jonah was in the belly of the whale for three days and three nights.
verses.kjv: For as Jonas was three days and three nights in the whale's belly; so shall the Son of man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.

verses.greek: ἄνδρες Νινευῖται ἀναστήσονται ἐν τῇ κρίσει μετὰ τῆς γενεᾶς ταύτης καὶ κατακρινοῦσιν αὐτήν· ὅτι μετενόησαν εἰς τὸ κήρυγμα Ἰωνᾶ, καὶ ἰδοὺ πλεῖον Ἰωνᾶ ὧδε.
verses.mistral: Men of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for they repented at the preaching of Jonah, and behold, more than Jonah is here.
verses.kjv: The men of Nineveh shall rise in judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: because they repented at the preaching of Jonas; and, behold, a greater than Jonas is here.

words.gloss: I raise up, set up, rise from

verses.greek: βασίλισσα νότου ἐγερθήσεται ἐν τῇ κρίσει μετὰ τῆς γενεᾶς ταύτης καὶ κατακρινεῖ αὐτήν· ὅτι ἦλθεν ἐκ τῶν περάτων τῆς γῆς ἀκοῦσαι τὴν σοφίαν Σολομῶνος, καὶ ἰδοὺ πλεῖον Σολομῶνος ὧδε.
verses.mistral: Queen of the south will rise at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and behold, more than Solomon is here.
verses.kjv: The queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for she came from the uttermost parts of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and, behold, a greater than Solomon is here.

verses.greek: Ὅταν δὲ τὸ ἀκάθαρτον πνεῦμα ἐξέλθῃ ἀπὸ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου, διέρχεται δι’ ἀνύδρων τόπων ζητοῦν ἀνάπαυσιν, καὶ οὐχ εὑρίσκει.
verses.mistral: When the unclean spirit leaves a man, it passes through arid places seeking rest but finds none.
verses.kjv: When the unclean spirit is gone out of a man, he walketh through dry places, seeking rest, and findeth none.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I pass through, spread a report

verses.greek: τότε λέγει· ⸂Εἰς τὸν οἶκόν μου ἐπιστρέψω⸃ ὅθεν ἐξῆλθον· καὶ ἐλθὸν ⸀εὑρίσκει ⸀σχολάζοντα σεσαρωμένον καὶ κεκοσμημένον.
verses.mistral: Returns to my house he goes, and finding it empty, swept and adorned.
verses.kjv: Then he saith, I will return into my house from whence I came out; and when he is come, he findeth it empty, swept, and garnished.

verses.greek: τότε πορεύεται καὶ παραλαμβάνει μεθ’ ἑαυτοῦ ἑπτὰ ἕτερα πνεύματα πονηρότερα ἑαυτοῦ, καὶ εἰσελθόντα κατοικεῖ ἐκεῖ· καὶ γίνεται τὰ ἔσχατα τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐκείνου χείρονα τῶν πρώτων. οὕτως ἔσται καὶ τῇ γενεᾷ ταύτῃ τῇ πονηρᾷ.
verses.mistral: Then he goes out and takes with him seven other spirits more wicked than himself, and when he enters, he dwells there; and the last state of that man becomes worse than the first. So it will be also with this wicked generation.
verses.kjv: Then goeth he, and taketh with himself seven other spirits more wicked than himself, and they enter in and dwell there: and the last state of that man is worse than the first. Even so shall it be also unto this wicked generation.

words.gloss: I go in, come in, enter
I dwell in, settle in, inhabit
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: Ἔτι ⸀δὲ αὐτοῦ λαλοῦντος τοῖς ὄχλοις ἰδοὺ ἡ μήτηρ καὶ οἱ ἀδελφοὶ αὐτοῦ εἱστήκεισαν ἔξω ζητοῦντες αὐτῷ λαλῆσαι.
verses.mistral: His mother and brothers are standing outside, seeking to speak with him.
verses.kjv: ¶ While he yet talked to the people, behold, his mother and his brethren stood without, desiring to speak with him.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ⸂εἶπεν δέ τις αὐτῷ· Ἰδοὺ ἡ μήτηρ σου καὶ οἱ ἀδελφοί σου ἔξω ἑστήκασιν, ζητοῦντές σοι λαλῆσαι.⸃
verses.mistral: His mother and brothers are standing outside, seeking to speak with him.
verses.kjv: Then one said unto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren stand without, desiring to speak with thee.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν τῷ ⸀λέγοντι αὐτῷ· Τίς ἐστιν ἡ μήτηρ μου, καὶ τίνες εἰσὶν οἱ ἀδελφοί μου;
verses.mistral: Who is my mother, and who are my brothers?
verses.kjv: But he answered and said unto him that told him, Who is my mother? and who are my brethren?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ ἐκτείνας τὴν χεῖρα αὐτοῦ ἐπὶ τοὺς μαθητὰς αὐτοῦ εἶπεν· Ἰδοὺ ἡ μήτηρ μου καὶ οἱ ἀδελφοί μου·
verses.mistral: Here are my mother and my brothers.
verses.kjv: And he stretched forth his hand toward his disciples, and said, Behold my mother and my brethren!

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: ὅστις γὰρ ἂν ποιήσῃ τὸ θέλημα τοῦ πατρός μου τοῦ ἐν οὐρανοῖς, αὐτός μου ἀδελφὸς καὶ ἀδελφὴ καὶ μήτηρ ἐστίν.
verses.mistral: Whoever does the will of my Father in heaven is my brother and sister and mother.
verses.kjv: For whosoever shall do the will of my Father which is in heaven, the same is my brother, and sister, and mother.

words.gloss: an untranslatable word that makes a statement contingent

verses.greek: ⸀Ἐν τῇ ἡμέρᾳ ἐκείνῃ ἐξελθὼν ὁ Ἰησοῦς ⸀τῆς οἰκίας ἐκάθητο παρὰ τὴν θάλασσαν·
verses.mistral: Sitting by the sea side, Jesus left the house
verses.kjv: The same day went Jesus out of the house, and sat by the sea side.

verses.greek: καὶ συνήχθησαν πρὸς αὐτὸν ὄχλοι πολλοί, ὥστε αὐτὸν ⸀εἰς πλοῖον ἐμβάντα καθῆσθαι, καὶ πᾶς ὁ ὄχλος ἐπὶ τὸν αἰγιαλὸν εἱστήκει.
verses.mistral: And great multitudes gathered to him, so that he went into a boat and sat down; and the whole crowd remained on the seashore.
verses.kjv: And great multitudes were gathered together unto him, so that he went into a ship, and sat; and the whole multitude stood on the shore.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: καὶ ἐλάλησεν αὐτοῖς πολλὰ ἐν παραβολαῖς λέγων· Ἰδοὺ ἐξῆλθεν ὁ σπείρων τοῦ σπείρειν.
verses.mistral: And he spoke to them in parables, saying: "Behold, the sower went out to sow."
verses.kjv: And he spake many things unto them in parables, saying, Behold, a sower went forth to sow;

verses.greek: καὶ ἐν τῷ σπείρειν αὐτὸν ἃ μὲν ἔπεσεν παρὰ τὴν ὁδόν, καὶ ⸂ἐλθόντα τὰ πετεινὰ⸃ κατέφαγεν αὐτά.
verses.mistral: And as he sowed, some seeds fell by the roadside, and the birds came and devoured them.
verses.kjv: And when he sowed, some seeds fell by the way side, and the fowls came and devoured them up:

words.gloss: I eat till it is finished

verses.greek: ἄλλα δὲ ἔπεσεν ἐπὶ τὰ πετρώδη ὅπου οὐκ εἶχεν γῆν πολλήν, καὶ εὐθέως ἐξανέτειλεν διὰ τὸ μὴ ἔχειν βάθος γῆς,
verses.mistral: Other seeds fell on rocky ground where there was not much soil, and immediately sprouted because it had no depth of soil.
verses.kjv: Some fell upon stony places, where they had not much earth: and forthwith they sprung up, because they had no deepness of earth:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: ἡλίου δὲ ἀνατείλαντος ἐκαυματίσθη καὶ διὰ τὸ μὴ ἔχειν ῥίζαν ἐξηράνθη.
verses.mistral: The sun rising caused them to be scorched, and since they had no root, they withered away.
verses.kjv: And when the sun was up, they were scorched; and because they had no root, they withered away.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I make to rise, I rise, shine

verses.greek: ἄλλα δὲ ἔπεσεν ἐπὶ τὰς ἀκάνθας, καὶ ἀνέβησαν αἱ ἄκανθαι καὶ ⸀ἔπνιξαν αὐτά.
verses.mistral: All fell upon the thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked them.
verses.kjv: And some fell among thorns; and the thorns sprung up, and choked them:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: ἄλλα δὲ ἔπεσεν ἐπὶ τὴν γῆν τὴν καλὴν καὶ ἐδίδου καρπόν, ὃ μὲν ἑκατὸν ὃ δὲ ἑξήκοντα ὃ δὲ τριάκοντα.
verses.mistral: Fell on the good ground and gave fruit, one hundredfold, sixtyfold, thirtyfold.
verses.kjv: But other fell into good ground, and brought forth fruit, some an hundredfold, some sixtyfold, some thirtyfold.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
on, to, against, on the basis of, at
but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὁ ἔχων ⸀ὦτα ἀκουέτω.
verses.mistral: Listening one (should) hear.
verses.kjv: Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.

verses.greek: Καὶ προσελθόντες οἱ μαθηταὶ εἶπαν αὐτῷ· Διὰ τί ἐν παραβολαῖς λαλεῖς αὐτοῖς;
verses.mistral: Speak to them in parables, why?
verses.kjv: And the disciples came, and said unto him, Why speakest thou unto them in parables?

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν ⸀αὐτοῖς· Ὅτι ὑμῖν δέδοται γνῶναι τὰ μυστήρια τῆς βασιλείας τῶν οὐρανῶν, ἐκείνοις δὲ οὐ δέδοται.
verses.mistral: Given response: To you it has been given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it has not been given.
verses.kjv: He answered and said unto them, Because it is given unto you to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it is not given.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come to know, learn, realize
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὅστις γὰρ ἔχει, δοθήσεται αὐτῷ καὶ περισσευθήσεται· ὅστις δὲ οὐκ ἔχει, καὶ ὃ ἔχει ἀρθήσεται ἀπ’ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Whoever has, will be given more, and he will have in abundance. Whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken from him.
verses.kjv: For whosoever hath, to him shall be given, and he shall have more abundance: but whosoever hath not, from him shall be taken away even that he hath.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: διὰ τοῦτο ἐν παραβολαῖς αὐτοῖς λαλῶ, ὅτι βλέποντες οὐ βλέπουσιν καὶ ἀκούοντες οὐκ ἀκούουσιν οὐδὲ συνίουσιν·
verses.mistral: I speak to them in parables, because seeing they do not see, and hearing they do not hear, nor understand.
verses.kjv: Therefore speak I to them in parables: because they seeing see not; and hearing they hear not, neither do they understand.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀναπληροῦται αὐτοῖς ἡ προφητεία Ἠσαΐου ἡ λέγουσα· Ἀκοῇ ἀκούσετε καὶ οὐ μὴ συνῆτε, καὶ βλέποντες βλέψετε καὶ οὐ μὴ ἴδητε.
verses.mistral: And is fulfilled to them the prophecy of Isaiah which says: Hear, you shall hear and not understand, and seeing you shall see and not perceive.
verses.kjv: And in them is fulfilled the prophecy of Esaias, which saith, By hearing ye shall hear, and shall not understand; and seeing ye shall see, and shall not perceive:

words.gloss: I fill up, make up, fulfill

verses.greek: ἐπαχύνθη γὰρ ἡ καρδία τοῦ λαοῦ τούτου, καὶ τοῖς ὠσὶν βαρέως ἤκουσαν, καὶ τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς αὐτῶν ἐκάμμυσαν· μήποτε ἴδωσιν τοῖς ὀφθαλμοῖς καὶ τοῖς ὠσὶν ἀκούσωσιν καὶ τῇ καρδίᾳ συνῶσιν καὶ ἐπιστρέψωσιν, καὶ ἰάσομαι αὐτούς.
verses.mistral: Hardens the heart of this people, and with their ears they heard heavily, and their eyes they shut; lest perhaps they see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and understand with their heart, and repent, and I heal them.
verses.kjv: For this people's heart is waxed gross, and their ears are dull of hearing, and their eyes they have closed; lest at any time they should see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and should understand with their heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them.

words.gloss: lest at any time, lest, perhaps

verses.greek: ὑμῶν δὲ μακάριοι οἱ ὀφθαλμοὶ ὅτι βλέπουσιν, καὶ τὰ ὦτα ὑμῶν ὅτι ⸀ἀκούουσιν.
verses.mistral: Blessed are the eyes that see and the ears that hear.
verses.kjv: But blessed are your eyes, for they see: and your ears, for they hear.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἀμὴν γὰρ λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι πολλοὶ προφῆται καὶ δίκαιοι ἐπεθύμησαν ἰδεῖν ἃ βλέπετε καὶ οὐκ εἶδαν, καὶ ἀκοῦσαι ἃ ἀκούετε καὶ οὐκ ἤκουσαν.
verses.mistral: Many prophets and righteous men longed to see what you see and hear what you hear, but did not see and hear.
verses.kjv: For verily I say unto you, That many prophets and righteous men have desired to see those things which ye see, and have not seen them; and to hear those things which ye hear, and have not heard them.

words.gloss: verily, truly, amen, so let it be
I long for, covet, lust after

verses.greek: Ὑμεῖς οὖν ἀκούσατε τὴν παραβολὴν τοῦ ⸀σπείραντος.
verses.mistral: You hear the parable of the sower.
verses.kjv: ¶ Hear ye therefore the parable of the sower.

verses.greek: παντὸς ἀκούοντος τὸν λόγον τῆς βασιλείας καὶ μὴ συνιέντος, ἔρχεται ὁ πονηρὸς καὶ ἁρπάζει τὸ ἐσπαρμένον ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ αὐτοῦ· οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ παρὰ τὴν ὁδὸν σπαρείς.
verses.mistral: The evil one comes and snatches away what was sown in the heart of the one who hears, but does not understand. This is the one sown along the path.
verses.kjv: When any one heareth the word of the kingdom, and understandeth it not, then cometh the wicked one, and catcheth away that which was sown in his heart. This is he which received seed by the way side.

words.gloss: I seize, snatch, obtain by robbery

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἐπὶ τὰ πετρώδη σπαρείς, οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ τὸν λόγον ἀκούων καὶ εὐθὺς μετὰ χαρᾶς λαμβάνων αὐτόν,
verses.mistral: The one on the rocky ground is he who hears the word and immediately with joy receives it.
verses.kjv: But he that received the seed into stony places, the same is he that heareth the word, and anon with joy receiveth it;

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: οὐκ ἔχει δὲ ῥίζαν ἐν ἑαυτῷ ἀλλὰ πρόσκαιρός ἐστιν, γενομένης δὲ θλίψεως ἢ διωγμοῦ διὰ τὸν λόγον εὐθὺς σκανδαλίζεται.
verses.mistral: He has no root in himself but is temporary. When affliction or persecution arises because of the word, immediately he is offended.
verses.kjv: Yet hath he not root in himself, but dureth for a while: for when tribulation or persecution ariseth because of the word, by and by he is offended.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come into being, am born
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ εἰς τὰς ἀκάνθας σπαρείς, οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ τὸν λόγον ἀκούων, καὶ ἡ μέριμνα τοῦ αἰῶνος ⸀τούτου καὶ ἡ ἀπάτη τοῦ πλούτου συμπνίγει τὸν λόγον, καὶ ἄκαρπος γίνεται.
verses.mistral: The one who hears the word among thorns, this is the man who the worry of the world and the deceitfulness of wealth choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.
verses.kjv: He also that received seed among the thorns is he that heareth the word; and the care of this world, and the deceitfulness of riches, choke the word, and he becometh unfruitful.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἐπὶ τὴν ⸂καλὴν γῆν⸃ σπαρείς, οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ τὸν λόγον ἀκούων καὶ ⸀συνιείς, ὃς δὴ καρποφορεῖ καὶ ποιεῖ ὃ μὲν ἑκατὸν ὃ δὲ ἑξήκοντα ὃ δὲ τριάκοντα.
verses.mistral: The one who hears the word and understands, this is the man who bears fruit and makes one hundred, another sixty, and another thirty.
verses.kjv: But he that received seed into the good ground is he that heareth the word, and understandeth it; which also beareth fruit, and bringeth forth, some an hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
on, to, against, on the basis of, at
but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Ἄλλην παραβολὴν παρέθηκεν αὐτοῖς λέγων· Ὡμοιώθη ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν ἀνθρώπῳ ⸀σπείραντι καλὸν σπέρμα ἐν τῷ ἀγρῷ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: The Kingdom of Heaven is likened to a man who sows good seed in his field.
verses.kjv: ¶ Another parable put he forth unto them, saying, The kingdom of heaven is likened unto a man which sowed good seed in his field:

words.gloss: I set before, serve, entrust to

verses.greek: ἐν δὲ τῷ καθεύδειν τοὺς ἀνθρώπους ἦλθεν αὐτοῦ ὁ ἐχθρὸς καὶ ⸀ἐπέσπειρεν ζιζάνια ἀνὰ μέσον τοῦ σίτου καὶ ἀπῆλθεν.
verses.mistral: En the night the enemy came and sowed tares among the wheat, and went away.
verses.kjv: But while men slept, his enemy came and sowed tares among the wheat, and went his way.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
and, apiece, by, each, every, in, through
I come or go away from, depart, return

verses.greek: ὅτε δὲ ἐβλάστησεν ὁ χόρτος καὶ καρπὸν ἐποίησεν, τότε ἐφάνη καὶ τὰ ζιζάνια.
verses.mistral: When the crop sprouted and produced grain, then the tares appeared.
verses.kjv: But when the blade was sprung up, and brought forth fruit, then appeared the tares also.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I sprout, cause to sprout, make to grow up

verses.greek: προσελθόντες δὲ οἱ δοῦλοι τοῦ οἰκοδεσπότου εἶπον αὐτῷ· Κύριε, οὐχὶ καλὸν σπέρμα ⸀ἔσπειρας ἐν τῷ σῷ ἀγρῷ; πόθεν οὖν ἔχει ζιζάνια;
verses.mistral: The servants approached their master and said to him, "Lord, did you not sow good seed in your field? Where then does the weeds come from?"
verses.kjv: So the servants of the householder came and said unto him, Sir, didst not thou sow good seed in thy field? from whence then hath it tares?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
by no means, not at all

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἔφη αὐτοῖς· Ἐχθρὸς ἄνθρωπος τοῦτο ἐποίησεν. οἱ δὲ ⸂δοῦλοι αὐτῷ λέγουσιν⸃· Θέλεις οὖν ἀπελθόντες ⸀συλλέξωμεν αὐτά;
verses.mistral: The master replied, "An enemy has done this." But the servants asked, "Do you want us then to go and gather them?"
verses.kjv: He said unto them, An enemy hath done this. The servants said unto him, Wilt thou then that we go and gather them up?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and
I come or go away from, depart, return

verses.greek: ὁ δέ ⸀φησιν· Οὔ, μήποτε συλλέγοντες τὰ ζιζάνια ἐκριζώσητε ἅμα αὐτοῖς τὸν σῖτον·
verses.mistral: Harvest the tares along with the wheat (Matthew 13:29, English Translation)
verses.kjv: But he said, Nay; lest while ye gather up the tares, ye root up also the wheat with them.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
lest at any time, lest, perhaps
at the same time, along with

verses.greek: ἄφετε συναυξάνεσθαι ἀμφότερα ⸀μέχρι τοῦ θερισμοῦ· καὶ ἐν καιρῷ τοῦ θερισμοῦ ἐρῶ τοῖς θερισταῖς· Συλλέξατε πρῶτον τὰ ζιζάνια καὶ δήσατε αὐτὰ εἰς δέσμας πρὸς τὸ κατακαῦσαι αὐτά, τὸν δὲ σῖτον ⸀συναγάγετε εἰς τὴν ἀποθήκην μου.
verses.mistral: Gather the bundles of tares and bind them in bundles to burn them, but gather the wheat into my barn.
verses.kjv: Let both grow together until the harvest: and in the time of harvest I will say to the reapers, Gather ye together first the tares, and bind them in bundles to burn them: but gather the wheat into my barn.

words.gloss: I send away, release, remit, forgive, permit
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Ἄλλην παραβολὴν παρέθηκεν αὐτοῖς λέγων· Ὁμοία ἐστὶν ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν κόκκῳ σινάπεως, ὃν λαβὼν ἄνθρωπος ἔσπειρεν ἐν τῷ ἀγρῷ αὐτοῦ·
verses.mistral: The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed, which a man took and sowed in his field.
verses.kjv: ¶ Another parable put he forth unto them, saying, The kingdom of heaven is like to a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and sowed in his field:

words.gloss: I set before, serve, entrust to

verses.greek: ὃ μικρότερον μέν ἐστιν πάντων τῶν σπερμάτων, ὅταν δὲ αὐξηθῇ μεῖζον τῶν λαχάνων ἐστὶν καὶ γίνεται δένδρον, ὥστε ἐλθεῖν τὰ πετεινὰ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ καὶ κατασκηνοῦν ἐν τοῖς κλάδοις αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Smaller is the mustard seed among all seeds, but when it grows, it becomes greater than the herbs and becomes a tree, so that the birds of the air come and lodge in its branches.
verses.kjv: Which indeed is the least of all seeds: but when it is grown, it is the greatest among herbs, and becometh a tree, so that the birds of the air come and lodge in the branches thereof.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I cause to increase, become greater, grow
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: Ἄλλην παραβολὴν ἐλάλησεν αὐτοῖς· Ὁμοία ἐστὶν ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν ζύμῃ, ἣν λαβοῦσα γυνὴ ⸀ἐνέκρυψεν εἰς ἀλεύρου σάτα τρία ἕως οὗ ἐζυμώθη ὅλον.
verses.mistral: The Kingdom of Heaven is like yeast that a woman took and hid in three measures of flour until it was all leavened.
verses.kjv: ¶ Another parable spake he unto them; The kingdom of heaven is like unto leaven, which a woman took, and hid in three measures of meal, till the whole was leavened.

verses.greek: Ταῦτα πάντα ἐλάλησεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς ἐν παραβολαῖς τοῖς ὄχλοις, καὶ χωρὶς παραβολῆς ⸀οὐδὲν ἐλάλει αὐτοῖς·
verses.mistral: All these things Jesus spoke to the crowds in parables, and without a parable He did not speak to them.
verses.kjv: All these things spake Jesus unto the multitude in parables; and without a parable spake he not unto them:

verses.greek: ὅπως πληρωθῇ τὸ ῥηθὲν διὰ τοῦ προφήτου λέγοντος· Ἀνοίξω ἐν παραβολαῖς τὸ στόμα μου, ἐρεύξομαι κεκρυμμένα ἀπὸ ⸀καταβολῆς.
verses.mistral: Opens in parables His mouth, I will disclose things hidden since the foundation of the world.
verses.kjv: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, saying, I will open my mouth in parables; I will utter things which have been kept secret from the foundation of the world.

verses.greek: Τότε ἀφεὶς τοὺς ὄχλους ἦλθεν εἰς τὴν ⸀οἰκίαν. καὶ προσῆλθον αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ λέγοντες· ⸀Διασάφησον ἡμῖν τὴν παραβολὴν τῶν ζιζανίων τοῦ ἀγροῦ.
verses.mistral: Then, leaving the crowds, He went into the house. And His disciples came to Him, saying, "Explain to us the parable of the tares of the field."
verses.kjv: Then Jesus sent the multitude away, and went into the house: and his disciples came unto him, saying, Declare unto us the parable of the tares of the field.

words.gloss: I send away, release, remit, forgive, permit

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς ⸀εἶπεν· Ὁ σπείρων τὸ καλὸν σπέρμα ἐστὶν ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου·
verses.mistral: The son of man is the one who sows the good seed.
verses.kjv: He answered and said unto them, He that soweth the good seed is the Son of man;

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀγρός ἐστιν ὁ κόσμος· τὸ δὲ καλὸν σπέρμα, οὗτοί εἰσιν οἱ υἱοὶ τῆς βασιλείας· τὰ δὲ ζιζάνιά εἰσιν οἱ υἱοὶ τοῦ πονηροῦ,
verses.mistral: The field is the world, but the good seed are the sons of the kingdom; the tares are the sons of the evil one.
verses.kjv: The field is the world; the good seed are the children of the kingdom; but the tares are the children of the wicked one;

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἐχθρὸς ὁ σπείρας αὐτά ἐστιν ὁ διάβολος· ὁ δὲ θερισμὸς συντέλεια ⸀αἰῶνός ἐστιν, οἱ δὲ θερισταὶ ἄγγελοί εἰσιν.
verses.mistral: The enemy who sows them is the devil; the harvest is the end of the age, and the reapers are angels.
verses.kjv: The enemy that sowed them is the devil; the harvest is the end of the world; and the reapers are the angels.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὥσπερ οὖν συλλέγεται τὰ ζιζάνια καὶ πυρὶ ⸀καίεται, οὕτως ἔσται ἐν τῇ συντελείᾳ τοῦ ⸀αἰῶνος·
verses.mistral: As the darnel is gathered and burned in the fire, so it will be in the end of the age.
verses.kjv: As therefore the tares are gathered and burned in the fire; so shall it be in the end of this world.

verses.greek: ἀποστελεῖ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου τοὺς ἀγγέλους αὐτοῦ, καὶ συλλέξουσιν ἐκ τῆς βασιλείας αὐτοῦ πάντα τὰ σκάνδαλα καὶ τοὺς ποιοῦντας τὴν ἀνομίαν,
verses.mistral: Sends the Son of Man his angels, and they will gather out of his kingdom all stumbling blocks and those doing lawlessness
verses.kjv: The Son of man shall send forth his angels, and they shall gather out of his kingdom all things that offend, and them which do iniquity;

words.gloss: I send forth, send, send away, dismiss

verses.greek: καὶ βαλοῦσιν αὐτοὺς εἰς τὴν κάμινον τοῦ πυρός· ἐκεῖ ἔσται ὁ κλαυθμὸς καὶ ὁ βρυγμὸς τῶν ὀδόντων.
verses.mistral: And they will cast them into the furnace of fire: there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth.
verses.kjv: And shall cast them into a furnace of fire: there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.

words.gloss: I cast, throw, rush, put, place, drop

verses.greek: Τότε οἱ δίκαιοι ἐκλάμψουσιν ὡς ὁ ἥλιος ἐν τῇ βασιλείᾳ τοῦ πατρὸς αὐτῶν. ὁ ἔχων ⸀ὦτα ἀκουέτω.
verses.mistral: Then the righteous will shine as the sun in the kingdom of their Father. He who has ears, let him hear.
verses.kjv: Then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father. Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.

verses.greek: ⸀Ὁμοία ἐστὶν ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν θησαυρῷ κεκρυμμένῳ ἐν τῷ ἀγρῷ, ὃν εὑρὼν ἄνθρωπος ἔκρυψεν, καὶ ἀπὸ τῆς χαρᾶς αὐτοῦ ὑπάγει καὶ ⸂πωλεῖ πάντα ὅσα ἔχει⸃ καὶ ἀγοράζει τὸν ἀγρὸν ἐκεῖνον.
verses.mistral: The kingdom of heaven is like a hidden treasure in a field, which a man found and hid again; then he goes and sells all that he has and buys that field.
verses.kjv: ¶ Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto treasure hid in a field; the which when a man hath found, he hideth, and for joy thereof goeth and selleth all that he hath, and buyeth that field.

verses.greek: Πάλιν ὁμοία ἐστὶν ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν ⸀ἀνθρώπῳ ἐμπόρῳ ζητοῦντι καλοὺς μαργαρίτας·
verses.mistral: A merchant seeking fine pearls.
verses.kjv: ¶ Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a merchant man, seeking goodly pearls:

words.gloss: again, further, on the other hand

verses.greek: ⸂εὑρὼν δὲ⸃ ἕνα πολύτιμον μαργαρίτην ἀπελθὼν πέπρακεν πάντα ὅσα εἶχεν καὶ ἠγόρασεν αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: Found a pearl of great value, he sold all he had and bought it.
verses.kjv: Who, when he had found one pearl of great price, went and sold all that he had, and bought it.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come or go away from, depart, return

verses.greek: Πάλιν ὁμοία ἐστὶν ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν σαγήνῃ βληθείσῃ εἰς τὴν θάλασσαν καὶ ἐκ παντὸς γένους συναγαγούσῃ·
verses.mistral: The kingdom of heaven is like a dragnet cast into the sea, gathering fish of every kind.
verses.kjv: ¶ Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a net, that was cast into the sea, and gathered of every kind:

words.gloss: again, further, on the other hand
I cast, throw, rush, put, place, drop

verses.greek: ἣν ὅτε ἐπληρώθη ἀναβιβάσαντες ἐπὶ τὸν αἰγιαλὸν καὶ καθίσαντες συνέλεξαν τὰ καλὰ εἰς ⸀ἄγγη, τὰ δὲ σαπρὰ ἔξω ἔβαλον.
verses.mistral: Gathered the good into vessels, but cast the bad away.
verses.kjv: Which, when it was full, they drew to shore, and sat down, and gathered the good into vessels, but cast the bad away.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at
but, on the other hand, and
I cast, throw, rush, put, place, drop

verses.greek: οὕτως ἔσται ἐν τῇ συντελείᾳ τοῦ αἰῶνος· ἐξελεύσονται οἱ ἄγγελοι καὶ ἀφοριοῦσιν τοὺς πονηροὺς ἐκ μέσου τῶν δικαίων
verses.mistral: Thus will be the end of the age: the angels shall come forth and gather out of the midst of the righteous the wicked.
verses.kjv: So shall it be at the end of the world: the angels shall come forth, and sever the wicked from among the just,

words.gloss: I rail off, separate, place apart

verses.greek: καὶ βαλοῦσιν αὐτοὺς εἰς τὴν κάμινον τοῦ πυρός· ἐκεῖ ἔσται ὁ κλαυθμὸς καὶ ὁ βρυγμὸς τῶν ὀδόντων.
verses.mistral: And they will throw them into the furnace of fire: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
verses.kjv: And shall cast them into the furnace of fire: there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.

words.gloss: I cast, throw, rush, put, place, drop

verses.greek: ⸀Συνήκατε ταῦτα πάντα; λέγουσιν αὐτῷ· ⸀Ναί.
verses.mistral: Gathered all these things? They said to Him, "Yes."
verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto them, Have ye understood all these things? They say unto him, Yea, Lord.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Διὰ τοῦτο πᾶς γραμματεὺς μαθητευθεὶς ⸂τῇ βασιλείᾳ⸃ τῶν οὐρανῶν ὅμοιός ἐστιν ἀνθρώπῳ οἰκοδεσπότῃ ὅστις ἐκβάλλει ἐκ τοῦ θησαυροῦ αὐτοῦ καινὰ καὶ παλαιά.
verses.mistral: The man is similar to a householder who brings out of his treasure both new and old.
verses.kjv: Then said he unto them, Therefore every scribe which is instructed unto the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that is an householder, which bringeth forth out of his treasure things new and old.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I make disciples, make into disciples
I throw, cast, put out, banish, bring forth, produce

verses.greek: καὶ ἐγένετο ὅτε ἐτέλεσεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς τὰς παραβολὰς ταύτας, μετῆρεν ἐκεῖθεν.
verses.mistral: And it came to pass when Jesus had finished these parables, He departed thence.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, that when Jesus had finished these parables, he departed thence.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born
I change my position, depart, remove

verses.greek: καὶ ἐλθὼν εἰς τὴν πατρίδα αὐτοῦ ἐδίδασκεν αὐτοὺς ἐν τῇ συναγωγῇ αὐτῶν, ὥστε ἐκπλήσσεσθαι αὐτοὺς καὶ λέγειν· Πόθεν τούτῳ ἡ σοφία αὕτη καὶ αἱ δυνάμεις;
verses.mistral: And coming into His own country, He taught them in their synagogue, so that they were astonished and said, "Where did this wisdom come from, and these miracles?"
verses.kjv: And when he was come into his own country, he taught them in their synagogue, insomuch that they were astonished, and said, Whence hath this man this wisdom, and these mighty works?

verses.greek: οὐχ οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ τοῦ τέκτονος υἱός; ⸀οὐχ ἡ μήτηρ αὐτοῦ λέγεται Μαριὰμ καὶ οἱ ἀδελφοὶ αὐτοῦ Ἰάκωβος καὶ ⸀Ἰωσὴφ καὶ Σίμων καὶ Ἰούδας;
verses.mistral: This man is not the carpenter's son? Is not his mother called Mary, and are not his brothers James and Joseph and Simon and Judas?
verses.kjv: Is not this the carpenter's son? is not his mother called Mary? and his brethren, James, and Joses, and Simon, and Judas?

verses.greek: καὶ αἱ ἀδελφαὶ αὐτοῦ οὐχὶ πᾶσαι πρὸς ἡμᾶς εἰσιν; πόθεν οὖν τούτῳ ταῦτα πάντα;
verses.mistral: And his sisters are not all here? Whence then to this one are these things?
verses.kjv: And his sisters, are they not all with us? Whence then hath this man all these things?

words.gloss: by no means, not at all

verses.greek: καὶ ἐσκανδαλίζοντο ἐν αὐτῷ. ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Οὐκ ἔστιν προφήτης ἄτιμος εἰ μὴ ἐν τῇ ⸀πατρίδι καὶ ἐν τῇ οἰκίᾳ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: And they took offense at him. But Jesus said to them, "A prophet is not without honor except in his own town and house."
verses.kjv: And they were offended in him. But Jesus said unto them, A prophet is not without honour, save in his own country, and in his own house.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ οὐκ ἐποίησεν ἐκεῖ δυνάμεις πολλὰς διὰ τὴν ἀπιστίαν αὐτῶν.
verses.mistral: Did not perform there many mighty works due to their unbelief.
verses.kjv: And he did not many mighty works there because of their unbelief.

verses.greek: Ἐν ἐκείνῳ τῷ καιρῷ ἤκουσεν Ἡρῴδης ὁ τετραάρχης τὴν ἀκοὴν Ἰησοῦ,
verses.mistral: Herod the tetrarch heard of Jesus
verses.kjv: At that time Herod the tetrarch heard of the fame of Jesus,

verses.greek: καὶ εἶπεν τοῖς παισὶν αὐτοῦ· Οὗτός ἐστιν Ἰωάννης ὁ βαπτιστής· αὐτὸς ἠγέρθη ἀπὸ τῶν νεκρῶν, καὶ διὰ τοῦτο αἱ δυνάμεις ἐνεργοῦσιν ἐν αὐτῷ.
verses.mistral: This one is John the Baptist; he has risen from the dead, and for this reason, the powers are working in him. [Matthew 14:2]
verses.kjv: And said unto his servants, This is John the Baptist; he is risen from the dead; and therefore mighty works do shew forth themselves in him.

verses.greek: Ὁ γὰρ Ἡρῴδης κρατήσας τὸν Ἰωάννην ἔδησεν ⸀αὐτὸν καὶ ⸂ἐν φυλακῇ ἀπέθετο⸃ διὰ Ἡρῳδιάδα τὴν γυναῖκα Φιλίππου τοῦ ἀδελφοῦ αὐτοῦ,
verses.mistral: Herod has imprisoned John and put him in custody on account of Herodias, the wife of Philip his brother.
verses.kjv: ¶ For Herod had laid hold on John, and bound him, and put him in prison for Herodias' sake, his brother Philip's wife.

words.gloss: I lay off or aside, renounce

verses.greek: ἔλεγεν γὰρ ⸂αὐτῷ ὁ Ἰωάννης⸃· Οὐκ ἔξεστίν σοι ἔχειν αὐτήν·
verses.mistral: John said to him, "It is not lawful for you to have her."
verses.kjv: For John said unto him, It is not lawful for thee to have her.

verses.greek: καὶ θέλων αὐτὸν ἀποκτεῖναι ἐφοβήθη τὸν ὄχλον, ὅτι ὡς προφήτην αὐτὸν εἶχον.
verses.mistral: Fearing the crowd, for he was regarded as a prophet.
verses.kjv: And when he would have put him to death, he feared the multitude, because they counted him as a prophet.

verses.greek: ⸂γενεσίοις δὲ γενομένοις⸃ τοῦ Ἡρῴδου ὠρχήσατο ἡ θυγάτηρ τῆς Ἡρῳδιάδος ἐν τῷ μέσῳ καὶ ἤρεσεν τῷ Ἡρῴδῃ,
verses.mistral: Births occurring to Herod, his daughter Herodias began to dance in the midst and pleased him.
verses.kjv: But when Herod's birthday was kept, the daughter of Herodias danced before them, and pleased Herod.

verses.mistral: He swore to her to give whatever she asks
verses.kjv: Whereupon he promised with an oath to give her whatsoever she would ask.

verses.greek: ἡ δὲ προβιβασθεῖσα ὑπὸ τῆς μητρὸς αὐτῆς· Δός μοι, φησίν, ὧδε ἐπὶ πίνακι τὴν κεφαλὴν Ἰωάννου τοῦ βαπτιστοῦ.
verses.mistral: Request here on this platter the head of John the Baptist.
verses.kjv: And she, being before instructed of her mother, said, Give me here John Baptist's head in a charger.

on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: καὶ ⸂λυπηθεὶς ὁ βασιλεὺς διὰ⸃ τοὺς ὅρκους καὶ τοὺς συνανακειμένους ἐκέλευσεν δοθῆναι,
verses.mistral: Saddened by the oaths, the king commanded that it be given
verses.kjv: And the king was sorry: nevertheless for the oath's sake, and them which sat with him at meat, he commanded it to be given her.

verses.greek: καὶ πέμψας ἀπεκεφάλισεν ⸀τὸν Ἰωάννην ἐν τῇ φυλακῇ·
verses.mistral: Sends and beheaded John in the prison.

verses.greek: καὶ ἠνέχθη ἡ κεφαλὴ αὐτοῦ ἐπὶ πίνακι καὶ ἐδόθη τῷ κορασίῳ, καὶ ἤνεγκεν τῇ μητρὶ αὐτῆς.
verses.mistral: Bringed was his head upon a platter and given to the servant, and he brought it to his mother.
verses.kjv: And his head was brought in a charger, and given to the damsel: and she brought it to her mother.

verses.mistral: And approaching his disciples took up the cloak and buried him, and coming they reported to Jesus.

verses.greek: ⸂Ἀκούσας δὲ⸃ ὁ Ἰησοῦς ἀνεχώρησεν ἐκεῖθεν ἐν πλοίῳ εἰς ἔρημον τόπον κατ’ ἰδίαν· καὶ ἀκούσαντες οἱ ὄχλοι ἠκολούθησαν αὐτῷ πεζῇ ἀπὸ τῶν πόλεων.
verses.mistral: Jesus heard about it and withdrew by boat to a remote place; and the crowds, hearing this, followed him on foot from the towns.

verses.mistral: Seeing a large crowd, he felt compassion for them and healed the sick among them.
verses.kjv: And Jesus went forth, and saw a great multitude, and was moved with compassion toward them, and he healed their sick.

verses.mistral: Evening came, and the disciples approached him saying, "This place is deserted, and the hour has already passed; dismiss the crowds so that they may go into the towns and buy food for themselves."
verses.kjv: ¶ And when it was evening, his disciples came to him, saying, This is a desert place, and the time is now past; send the multitude away, that they may go into the villages, and buy themselves victuals.

verses.mistral: Jesus said to them, "They need not go away; give them something to eat yourselves."

The disciples in turn gave them to the crowds.
verses.kjv: And he commanded the multitude to sit down on the grass, and took the five loaves, and the two fishes, and looking up to heaven, he blessed, and brake, and gave the loaves to his disciples, and the disciples to the multitude.

verses.mistral: And they all ate and were satisfied, and they took up the fragments remaining twelve baskets full.
verses.kjv: And they did all eat, and were filled: and they took up of the fragments that remained twelve baskets full.

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ ἐσθίοντες ἦσαν ἄνδρες ὡσεὶ πεντακισχίλιοι χωρὶς γυναικῶν καὶ παιδίων.

verses.kjv: ¶ And straightway Jesus constrained his disciples to get into a ship, and to go before him unto the other side, while he sent the multitudes away.

verses.kjv: And when he had sent the multitudes away, he went up into a mountain apart to pray: and when the evening was come, he was there alone.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.mistral: And the men of that place recognized Him, and they sent throughout all the surrounding region, and brought to Him all who were sick.

verses.mistral: They asked Him to only touch the hem of His garment, and those who touched were healed.
verses.kjv: And besought him that they might only touch the hem of his garment: and as many as touched were made perfectly whole.

verses.greek: Τότε προσέρχονται τῷ ⸀Ἰησοῦ ἀπὸ Ἱεροσολύμων ⸂Φαρισαῖοι καὶ γραμματεῖς⸃ λέγοντες
verses.mistral: Then come to Jesus from Jerusalem Pharisees and scribes, saying

verses.greek: Διὰ τί οἱ μαθηταί σου παραβαίνουσιν τὴν παράδοσιν τῶν πρεσβυτέρων; οὐ γὰρ νίπτονται τὰς χεῖρας ⸀αὐτῶν ὅταν ἄρτον ἐσθίωσιν.
verses.mistral: Your disciples break the tradition of the elders, for they do not wash their hands when eating bread.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Διὰ τί καὶ ὑμεῖς παραβαίνετε τὴν ἐντολὴν τοῦ θεοῦ διὰ τὴν παράδοσιν ὑμῶν;
verses.mistral: But He answering them said, 'Why do you also transgress the commandment of God because of your tradition?'
verses.kjv: But he answered and said unto them, Why do ye also transgress the commandment of God by your tradition?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὁ γὰρ θεὸς ⸀εἶπεν· Τίμα τὸν πατέρα καὶ τὴν μητέρα, καί· Ὁ κακολογῶν πατέρα ἢ μητέρα θανάτῳ τελευτάτω·
verses.mistral: Honors father and mother is the commandment
verses.kjv: For God commanded, saying, Honour thy father and mother: and, He that curseth father or mother, let him die the death.

verses.mistral: Honors not his father, and you have set aside the word of God by your tradition.

I make unclean, regard as unclean

verses.greek: ἐκ γὰρ τῆς καρδίας ἐξέρχονται διαλογισμοὶ πονηροί, φόνοι, μοιχεῖαι, πορνεῖαι, κλοπαί, ψευδομαρτυρίαι, βλασφημίαι.
verses.mistral: From the heart come evil thoughts, murder, adultery, sexual immorality, theft, false witness, blasphemy.
verses.kjv: For out of the heart proceed evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, fornications, thefts, false witness, blasphemies:

verses.greek: ταῦτά ἐστιν τὰ κοινοῦντα τὸν ἄνθρωπον, τὸ δὲ ἀνίπτοις χερσὶν φαγεῖν οὐ κοινοῖ τὸν ἄνθρωπον.
verses.mistral: These are the things that defile a man, but eating with unwashed hands does not defile a man.
verses.kjv: These are the things which defile a man: but to eat with unwashen hands defileth not a man.

words.gloss: I make unclean, regard as unclean
but, on the other hand, and
I make unclean, regard as unclean

verses.greek: Καὶ ἐξελθὼν ἐκεῖθεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς ἀνεχώρησεν εἰς τὰ μέρη Τύρου καὶ Σιδῶνος.
verses.mistral: Jesus departed thence and went into the regions of Tyre and Sidon.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Jesus went thence, and departed into the coasts of Tyre and Sidon.

verses.greek: καὶ ἰδοὺ γυνὴ Χαναναία ἀπὸ τῶν ὁρίων ἐκείνων ἐξελθοῦσα ⸀ἔκραζεν λέγουσα· Ἐλέησόν με, κύριε ⸀υἱὸς Δαυίδ· ἡ θυγάτηρ μου κακῶς δαιμονίζεται.
verses.mistral: A Canaanite woman from that region came out and began saying, "Have mercy on me, Lord, Son of David! My daughter is tormented by a demon."
verses.kjv: And, behold, a woman of Canaan came out of the same coasts, and cried unto him, saying, Have mercy on me, O Lord, thou Son of David; my daughter is grievously vexed with a devil.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ οὐκ ἀπεκρίθη αὐτῇ λόγον. καὶ προσελθόντες οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ ⸀ἠρώτουν αὐτὸν λέγοντες· Ἀπόλυσον αὐτήν, ὅτι κράζει ὄπισθεν ἡμῶν.
verses.mistral: He did not answer her a word. And approaching, his disciples asked him, "Release her, for she is crying out behind us."
verses.kjv: But he answered her not a word. And his disciples came and besought him, saying, Send her away; for she crieth after us.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I release, let go, send away, divorce

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν· Οὐκ ἀπεστάλην εἰ μὴ εἰς τὰ πρόβατα τὰ ἀπολωλότα οἴκου Ἰσραήλ.
verses.mistral: He replied and said, "I have not come except to the lost sheep of the house of Israel."
verses.kjv: But he answered and said, I am not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I send forth, send, send away, dismiss

verses.greek: ἡ δὲ ἐλθοῦσα ⸀προσεκύνει αὐτῷ λέγουσα· Κύριε, βοήθει μοι.
verses.mistral: She approaches and kneels before him, saying: "Lord, help me."
verses.kjv: Then came she and worshipped him, saying, Lord, help me.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come to the rescue of

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν· Οὐκ ἔστιν καλὸν λαβεῖν τὸν ἄρτον τῶν τέκνων καὶ βαλεῖν τοῖς κυναρίοις.
verses.mistral: It is not right to take the children's bread and toss it to the dogs.
verses.kjv: But he answered and said, It is not meet to take the children's bread, and to cast it to dogs.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I cast, throw, rush, put, place, drop

verses.greek: ἡ δὲ εἶπεν· Ναί, κύριε, καὶ γὰρ τὰ κυνάρια ἐσθίει ἀπὸ τῶν ψιχίων τῶν πιπτόντων ἀπὸ τῆς τραπέζης τῶν κυρίων αὐτῶν.
verses.mistral: Yes, Lord; even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall from their masters' table.
verses.kjv: And she said, Truth, Lord: yet the dogs eat of the crumbs which fall from their masters' table.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: τότε ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτῇ· Ὦ γύναι, μεγάλη σου ἡ πίστις· γενηθήτω σοι ὡς θέλεις. καὶ ἰάθη ἡ θυγάτηρ αὐτῆς ἀπὸ τῆς ὥρας ἐκείνης.
verses.mistral: Then Jesus replied to her, "Daughter, your faith is great; let it be as you wish." And her daughter was healed from that very hour.
verses.kjv: Then Jesus answered and said unto her, O woman, great is thy faith: be it unto thee even as thou wilt. And her daughter was made whole from that very hour.

verses.greek: Καὶ μεταβὰς ἐκεῖθεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς ἦλθεν παρὰ τὴν θάλασσαν τῆς Γαλιλαίας, καὶ ἀναβὰς εἰς τὸ ὄρος ἐκάθητο ἐκεῖ.
verses.mistral: And from there Jesus went away to the sea of Galilee, and going up on the mountain He sat there.
verses.kjv: And Jesus departed from thence, and came nigh unto the sea of Galilee; and went up into a mountain, and sat down there.

words.gloss: I leave, depart, remove, pass over

verses.greek: καὶ προσῆλθον αὐτῷ ὄχλοι πολλοὶ ἔχοντες μεθ’ ἑαυτῶν ⸂κωφούς, τυφλούς, χωλούς, κυλλούς⸃, καὶ ἑτέρους πολλούς, καὶ ἔρριψαν αὐτοὺς παρὰ τοὺς πόδας ⸀αὐτοῦ, καὶ ἐθεράπευσεν αὐτούς·
verses.mistral: And a great crowd came to Him, having with them the lame, blind, crippled, maimed, and many others; and they threw them at His feet, and He healed them.
verses.kjv: And great multitudes came unto him, having with them those that were lame, blind, dumb, maimed, and many others, and cast them down at Jesus' feet; and he healed them:

words.gloss: I care for, attend, serve, heal

verses.greek: ὥστε ⸂τὸν ὄχλον⸃ θαυμάσαι βλέποντας κωφοὺς λαλοῦντας ⸂κυλλοὺς ὑγιεῖς⸃ ⸀καὶ χωλοὺς περιπατοῦντας καὶ τυφλοὺς βλέποντας· καὶ ἐδόξασαν τὸν θεὸν Ἰσραήλ.
verses.mistral: The crowd marveled, seeing mute men speaking, paralytics walking, and blind men seeing; and they glorified the God of Israel.
verses.kjv: Insomuch that the multitude wondered, when they saw the dumb to speak, the maimed to be whole, the lame to walk, and the blind to see: and they glorified the God of Israel.

words.gloss: I glorify, honor, bestow glory on

verses.greek: Ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς προσκαλεσάμενος τοὺς μαθητὰς αὐτοῦ εἶπεν· Σπλαγχνίζομαι ἐπὶ τὸν ὄχλον, ὅτι ἤδη ἡμέραι τρεῖς προσμένουσίν μοι καὶ οὐκ ἔχουσιν τί φάγωσιν· καὶ ἀπολῦσαι αὐτοὺς νήστεις οὐ θέλω, μήποτε ἐκλυθῶσιν ἐν τῇ ὁδῷ.
verses.mistral: Jesus called his disciples and said to them, "I feel compassion for the crowd, because they have been with me now for three days and have nothing to eat. I do not want to send them away hungry, or they might collapse on the way."
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Jesus called his disciples unto him, and said, I have compassion on the multitude, because they continue with me now three days, and have nothing to eat: and I will not send them away fasting, lest they faint in the way.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ λέγουσιν αὐτῷ οἱ ⸀μαθηταί· Πόθεν ἡμῖν ἐν ἐρημίᾳ ἄρτοι τοσοῦτοι ὥστε χορτάσαι ὄχλον τοσοῦτον;
verses.mistral: Where can we in the wilderness find such large amounts of bread to feed this vast crowd?
verses.kjv: And his disciples say unto him, Whence should we have so much bread in the wilderness, as to fill so great a multitude?

verses.greek: καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς· Πόσους ἄρτους ἔχετε; οἱ δὲ εἶπαν· Ἑπτά, καὶ ὀλίγα ἰχθύδια.
verses.mistral: And Jesus said to them, "How many loaves do you have?" But they said, "Seven, and a few small fish."
verses.kjv: And Jesus saith unto them, How many loaves have ye? And they said, Seven, and a few little fishes.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀παραγγείλας ⸂τῷ ὄχλῳ⸃ ἀναπεσεῖν ἐπὶ τὴν γῆν
verses.mistral: Command the crowd to recline on the ground
verses.kjv: And he commanded the multitude to sit down on the ground.

words.gloss: I lie down, recline, fall back upon
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: ⸀ἔλαβεν τοὺς ἑπτὰ ἄρτους καὶ τοὺς ἰχθύας ⸀καὶ εὐχαριστήσας ἔκλασεν καὶ ⸀ἐδίδου τοῖς ⸀μαθηταῖς οἱ δὲ μαθηταὶ ⸂τοῖς ὄχλοις⸃.
verses.mistral: Feed the seven loaves and the fish, and after giving thanks He broke them and gave to His disciples. But the disciples gave to the multitudes.
verses.kjv: And he took the seven loaves and the fishes, and gave thanks, and brake them, and gave to his disciples, and the disciples to the multitude.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ ἔφαγον πάντες καὶ ἐχορτάσθησαν, καὶ ⸂τὸ περισσεῦον τῶν κλασμάτων ἦραν⸃ ἑπτὰ σπυρίδας πλήρεις.
verses.mistral: And they all ate and were satisfied, and they took up the fragments remaining, seven large baskets full.
verses.kjv: And they did all eat, and were filled: and they took up of the broken meat that was left seven baskets full.

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ ἐσθίοντες ἦσαν τετρακισχίλιοι ἄνδρες χωρὶς γυναικῶν καὶ παιδίων.
verses.mistral: The men, numbering four thousand, were dining, without women and children.
verses.kjv: And they that did eat were four thousand men, beside women and children.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ ἀπολύσας τοὺς ὄχλους ἐνέβη εἰς τὸ πλοῖον, καὶ ἦλθεν εἰς τὰ ὅρια ⸀Μαγαδάν.
verses.mistral: Left the crowds, entered into the boat, and went to Magadan's borders.
verses.kjv: And he sent away the multitude, and took ship, and came into the coasts of Magdala.

words.gloss: I release, let go, send away, divorce

verses.greek: Καὶ προσελθόντες οἱ Φαρισαῖοι καὶ Σαδδουκαῖοι πειράζοντες ἐπηρώτησαν αὐτὸν σημεῖον ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ ἐπιδεῖξαι αὐτοῖς.
verses.mistral: And approaching, the Pharisees and Sadducees, testing, asked Him to show them a sign from heaven.
verses.kjv: The Pharisees also with the Sadducees came, and tempting desired him that he would shew them a sign from heaven.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· ⸂Ὀψίας γενομένης λέγετε· Εὐδία, πυρράζει γὰρ ὁ οὐρανός·
verses.mistral: Evening has come, and you say, "Look, Elijah is coming."
verses.kjv: He answered and said unto them, When it is evening, ye say, It will be fair weather: for the sky is red.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: καὶ πρωΐ· Σήμερον χειμών, πυρράζει γὰρ στυγνάζων ὁ οὐρανός. ⸀τὸ μὲν πρόσωπον τοῦ οὐρανοῦ γινώσκετε διακρίνειν, τὰ δὲ σημεῖα τῶν καιρῶν οὐ δύνασθε.⸃
verses.mistral: Morning came, and lo, a tempest, for the heavens are sighing with violence. Indeed, you can discern the countenance of the heavens, but the signs of the times you cannot.
verses.kjv: And in the morning, It will be foul weather to day: for the sky is red and lowring. O ye hypocrites, ye can discern the face of the sky; but can ye not discern the signs of the times?

words.gloss: early in the morning, at dawn
I come to know, learn, realize
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Γενεὰ πονηρὰ καὶ μοιχαλὶς σημεῖον ἐπιζητεῖ, καὶ σημεῖον οὐ δοθήσεται αὐτῇ εἰ μὴ τὸ σημεῖον ⸀Ἰωνᾶ. καὶ καταλιπὼν αὐτοὺς ἀπῆλθεν.
verses.mistral: Evil generation seeks after a sign, and a sign will not be given to it except the sign of Jonah. And leaving them, He went away.
verses.kjv: A wicked and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given unto it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas. And he left them, and departed.

words.gloss: I seek after, desire, search for
I come or go away from, depart, return
