verses.mistral: And coming over, the disciples forgot to take bread with them.
verses.kjv: And when his disciples were come to the other side, they had forgotten to take bread.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Ὁρᾶτε καὶ προσέχετε ἀπὸ τῆς ζύμης τῶν Φαρισαίων καὶ Σαδδουκαίων.
verses.mistral: Jesus said to them, "Observe and follow the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees."
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Jesus said unto them, Take heed and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees.

I pay attention to, devote myself to

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ διελογίζοντο ἐν ἑαυτοῖς λέγοντες ὅτι Ἄρτους οὐκ ἐλάβομεν.
verses.mistral: They said to themselves, "We did not take bread."
verses.kjv: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, It is because we have taken no bread.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: γνοὺς δὲ ὁ Ἰησοῦς ⸀εἶπεν· Τί διαλογίζεσθε ἐν ἑαυτοῖς, ὀλιγόπιστοι, ὅτι ἄρτους οὐκ ⸀ἐλάβετε;
verses.mistral: Realizing then Jesus said to them, "Why are you discussing among yourselves that you did not take bread?"
verses.kjv: Which when Jesus perceived, he said unto them, O ye of little faith, why reason ye among yourselves, because ye have brought no bread?

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: οὔπω νοεῖτε, οὐδὲ μνημονεύετε τοὺς πέντε ἄρτους τῶν πεντακισχιλίων καὶ πόσους κοφίνους ἐλάβετε;
verses.mistral: Have you not yet understood, or remembered the five loaves of the fifty thousand and how many baskets you took?
verses.kjv: Do ye not yet understand, neither remember the five loaves of the five thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?

verses.greek: οὐδὲ τοὺς ἑπτὰ ἄρτους τῶν τετρακισχιλίων καὶ πόσας σπυρίδας ἐλάβετε;
verses.mistral: Fourteen baskets how many did you take of the seven loaves of the four thousand?
verses.kjv: Neither the seven loaves of the four thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?

verses.greek: πῶς οὐ νοεῖτε ὅτι οὐ περὶ ⸀ἄρτων εἶπον ὑμῖν; ⸂προσέχετε δὲ⸃ ἀπὸ τῆς ζύμης τῶν Φαρισαίων καὶ Σαδδουκαίων.
verses.mistral: You do not understand that I did not speak to you about bread? But beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
verses.kjv: How is it that ye do not understand that I spake it not to you concerning bread, that ye should beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees?

words.gloss: I pay attention to, devote myself to
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: τότε συνῆκαν ὅτι οὐκ εἶπεν προσέχειν ἀπὸ τῆς ζύμης ⸂τῶν ἄρτων⸃ ἀλλὰ ἀπὸ τῆς διδαχῆς τῶν Φαρισαίων καὶ Σαδδουκαίων.
verses.mistral: Then they understood that He spoke not about the bread, but about the doctrine of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
verses.kjv: Then understood they how that he bade them not beware of the leaven of bread, but of the doctrine of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees.

words.gloss: I pay attention to, devote myself to

verses.greek: Ἐλθὼν δὲ ὁ Ἰησοῦς εἰς τὰ μέρη Καισαρείας τῆς Φιλίππου ἠρώτα τοὺς μαθητὰς αὐτοῦ λέγων· ⸀Τίνα λέγουσιν οἱ ἄνθρωποι εἶναι τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου;
verses.mistral: Who do people say that the Son of Man is?
verses.kjv: ¶ When Jesus came into the coasts of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, saying, Whom do men say that I the Son of man am?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ εἶπαν· Οἱ μὲν Ἰωάννην τὸν βαπτιστήν, ἄλλοι δὲ Ἠλίαν, ἕτεροι δὲ Ἰερεμίαν ἢ ἕνα τῶν προφητῶν.
verses.mistral: They replied, "Some say John the Baptist, others say Elijah, and still others Jeremiah or one of the prophets."
verses.kjv: And they said, Some say that thou art John the Baptist: some, Elias; and others, Jeremias, or one of the prophets.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: λέγει αὐτοῖς· Ὑμεῖς δὲ τίνα με λέγετε εἶναι;
verses.mistral: You say who I am?
verses.kjv: He saith unto them, But whom say ye that I am?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ Σίμων Πέτρος εἶπεν· Σὺ εἶ ὁ χριστὸς ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ θεοῦ τοῦ ζῶντος.
verses.mistral: You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.
verses.kjv: And Simon Peter answered and said, Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ⸂ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ⸃ ὁ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτῷ· Μακάριος εἶ, Σίμων Βαριωνᾶ, ὅτι σὰρξ καὶ αἷμα οὐκ ἀπεκάλυψέν σοι ἀλλ’ ὁ πατήρ μου ὁ ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς·
verses.mistral: Blessed are you, Simon Bar-Jonah, for flesh and blood has not revealed this to you, but my Father who is in heaven.
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto him, Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona: for flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee, but my Father which is in heaven.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I uncover, bring to light, reveal

verses.greek: κἀγὼ δέ σοι λέγω ὅτι σὺ εἶ Πέτρος, καὶ ἐπὶ ταύτῃ τῇ πέτρᾳ οἰκοδομήσω μου τὴν ἐκκλησίαν, καὶ πύλαι ᾅδου οὐ κατισχύσουσιν αὐτῆς·
verses.mistral: Upon you I will build my church, and the gates of Hades shall not prevail against it.
verses.kjv: And I say also unto thee, That thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church; and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
on, to, against, on the basis of, at
I prevail against, overpower, am able

verses.greek: ⸀δώσω σοι τὰς ⸀κλεῖδας τῆς βασιλείας τῶν οὐρανῶν, καὶ ὃ ⸀ἐὰν δήσῃς ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς ἔσται δεδεμένον ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς, καὶ ὃ ⸁ἐὰν λύσῃς ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς ἔσται λελυμένον ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς.
verses.mistral: I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven.
verses.kjv: And I will give unto thee the keys of the kingdom of heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt bind on earth shall be bound in heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: τότε ⸀διεστείλατο τοῖς ⸀μαθηταῖς ἵνα μηδενὶ εἴπωσιν ὅτι αὐτός ⸀ἐστιν ὁ χριστός.
verses.mistral: Then he commanded his disciples to tell no one that he was the Christ.
verses.kjv: Then charged he his disciples that they should tell no man that he was Jesus the Christ.

verses.greek: Ἀπὸ τότε ἤρξατο ⸂ὁ Ἰησοῦς⸃ δεικνύειν τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ ὅτι δεῖ αὐτὸν ⸂εἰς Ἱεροσόλυμα ἀπελθεῖν⸃ καὶ πολλὰ παθεῖν ἀπὸ τῶν πρεσβυτέρων καὶ ἀρχιερέων καὶ γραμματέων καὶ ἀποκτανθῆναι καὶ τῇ τρίτῃ ἡμέρᾳ ἐγερθῆναι.
verses.mistral: Jesus began to show his disciples that it was necessary for him to go to Jerusalem, and to suffer many things from the elders and chief priests and scribes, and to be killed, and on the third day to be raised.
verses.kjv: ¶ From that time forth began Jesus to shew unto his disciples, how that he must go unto Jerusalem, and suffer many things of the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and be raised again the third day.

words.gloss: I come or go away from, depart, return

verses.greek: καὶ προσλαβόμενος αὐτὸν ὁ Πέτρος ἤρξατο ἐπιτιμᾶν αὐτῷ λέγων· Ἵλεώς σοι, κύριε· οὐ μὴ ἔσται σοι τοῦτο.
verses.mistral: And Peter taking him began to rebuke him, saying, "Far from you, Lord! This shall not be to you."
verses.kjv: Then Peter took him, and began to rebuke him, saying, Be it far from thee, Lord: this shall not be unto thee.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ στραφεὶς εἶπεν τῷ Πέτρῳ· Ὕπαγε ὀπίσω μου, Σατανᾶ· σκάνδαλον ⸂εἶ ἐμοῦ⸃, ὅτι οὐ φρονεῖς τὰ τοῦ θεοῦ ἀλλὰ τὰ τῶν ἀνθρώπων.
verses.mistral: Follow me, Peter, Satan! You are a stumbling block to me, because you are not thinking God's thoughts but human ones.
verses.kjv: But he turned, and said unto Peter, Get thee behind me, Satan: thou art an offence unto me: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but those that be of men.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Τότε ὁ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ· Εἴ τις θέλει ὀπίσω μου ἐλθεῖν, ἀπαρνησάσθω ἑαυτὸν καὶ ἀράτω τὸν σταυρὸν αὐτοῦ καὶ ἀκολουθείτω μοι.
verses.mistral: Then Jesus said to His disciples, "If anyone wants to follow Me, let him deny himself, take up his cross, and follow Me."
verses.kjv: ¶ Then said Jesus unto his disciples, If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.

verses.greek: ὃς γὰρ ⸀ἐὰν θέλῃ τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ σῶσαι ἀπολέσει αὐτήν· ὃς δ’ ἂν ἀπολέσῃ τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ ἕνεκεν ἐμοῦ εὑρήσει αὐτήν.
verses.mistral: Whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for My sake will find it.
verses.kjv: For whosoever will save his life shall lose it: and whosoever will lose his life for my sake shall find it.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.mistral: If your hand or your foot causes you to stumble, cut it off and throw it away. It is better for you to enter life crippled or lame, rather than having two hands or two feet and being thrown into eternal fire.
verses.kjv: Wherefore if thy hand or thy foot offend thee, cut them off, and cast them from thee: it is better for thee to enter into life halt or maimed, rather than having two hands or two feet to be cast into everlasting fire.

verses.greek: Ἐὰν δὲ ἁμαρτήσῃ ⸂εἰς σὲ⸃ ὁ ἀδελφός σου, ⸀ὕπαγε ἔλεγξον αὐτὸν μεταξὺ σοῦ καὶ αὐτοῦ μόνου. ἐάν σου ἀκούσῃ, ἐκέρδησας τὸν ἀδελφόν σου·
verses.mistral: If your brother sins against you, go and reprove him privately; if he listens to you, you have gained your brother.

verses.mistral: If he does not listen, take with you one or two more, so that on the testimony of two or three witnesses every word may be established.

verses.mistral: You shall sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Verily I say unto you, That ye which have followed me, in the regeneration when the Son of man shall sit in the throne of his glory, ye also shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

verses.kjv: And said unto them; Go ye also into the vineyard, and whatsoever is right I will give you. And they went their way.

verses.mistral: They tell him, "Nobody has hired us." He says to them, "Go, you also, into the vineyard."
verses.kjv: They say unto him, Because no man hath hired us. He saith unto them, Go ye also into the vineyard; and whatsoever is right, that shall ye receive.

verses.mistral: The Lord of the vineyard says to his overseer in the evening, "Call the laborers and give them their wages, starting from the last ones up to the first."
verses.kjv: So when even was come, the lord of the vineyard saith unto his steward, Call the labourers, and give them their hire, beginning from the last unto the first.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτῇ· Τί θέλεις; λέγει αὐτῷ· Εἰπὲ ἵνα καθίσωσιν οὗτοι οἱ δύο υἱοί μου εἷς ἐκ δεξιῶν ⸀σου καὶ εἷς ἐξ εὐωνύμων σου ἐν τῇ βασιλείᾳ σου.
verses.mistral: He said to her, "What do you want?" She said to him, "Say that these two sons of mine may sit, one on your right and one on your left in your kingdom."
verses.kjv: And he said unto her, What wilt thou? She saith unto him, Grant that these my two sons may sit, the one on thy right hand, and the other on the left, in thy kingdom.

verses.kjv: But Jesus answered and said, Ye know not what ye ask. Are ye able to drink of the cup that I shall drink of, and to be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with? They say unto him, We are able.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.mistral: You shall drink from my cup, but sitting at my right hand and from my side is not mine to give, but it is for those for whom it has been prepared by my Father.
verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, Ye shall drink indeed of my cup, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with: but to sit on my right hand, and on my left, is not mine to give, but it shall be given to them for whom it is prepared of my Father.

verses.mistral: The largest crowd spread their garments on the road, others cut branches from the trees and spread them on the road.
verses.kjv: And a very great multitude spread their garments in the way; others cut down branches from the trees, and strawed them in the way.

verses.mistral: Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord! Blessed be the coming kingdom of our father David! Hosanna in the highest!
verses.kjv: And the multitudes that went before, and that followed, cried, saying, Hosanna to the Son of David: Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord; Hosanna in the highest.

verses.mistral: Who is this?

verses.mistral: Jesus entered the temple and drove out all those who were buying and selling in the temple, and overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who were selling pigeons.

verses.mistral: My house shall be called a house of prayer, but you have made it a den of robbers.

verses.mistral: The religious leaders and teachers, seeing the miracles He performed and hearing the children crying out in the temple and saying, "Hosanna to the Son of David," were indignant

verses.greek: καὶ ἰδὼν συκῆν μίαν ἐπὶ τῆς ὁδοῦ ἦλθεν ἐπ’ αὐτήν, καὶ οὐδὲν εὗρεν ἐν αὐτῇ εἰ μὴ φύλλα μόνον, καὶ λέγει αὐτῇ· ⸀Μηκέτι ἐκ σοῦ καρπὸς γένηται εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα· καὶ ἐξηράνθη παραχρῆμα ἡ συκῆ.
verses.mistral: The sycamore tree had no fruit except leaves. He said to it, "May you never produce fruit again for the future." Immediately, the sycamore withered.
verses.kjv: And when he saw a fig tree in the way, he came to it, and found nothing thereon, but leaves only, and said unto it, Let no fruit grow on thee henceforward for ever. And presently the fig tree withered away.

verses.mistral: Immediately, the disciples marveled, saying, "How did the sycamore tree wither so quickly?"
verses.kjv: And when the disciples saw it, they marvelled, saying, How soon is the fig tree withered away!

verses.greek: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν, ἐὰν ἔχητε πίστιν καὶ μὴ διακριθῆτε, οὐ μόνον τὸ τῆς συκῆς ποιήσετε, ἀλλὰ κἂν τῷ ὄρει τούτῳ εἴπητε· Ἄρθητι καὶ βλήθητι εἰς τὴν θάλασσαν, γενήσεται·
verses.mistral: Jesus replied to them and said to them, "Truly I tell you, if you have faith and do not doubt, not only will you do what was done to the sycamore tree, but even if you say to this mountain, 'Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,' it will be done.
verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto them, Verily I say unto you, If ye have faith, and doubt not, ye shall not only do this which is done to the fig tree, but also if ye shall say unto this mountain, Be thou removed, and be thou cast into the sea; it shall be done.

verses.mistral: They approached Him while He was in the temple teaching, and the chief priests and elders of the people said to Him, "By what authority are you doing these things? And who gave you this authority?"
verses.kjv: ¶ And when he was come into the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people came unto him as he was teaching, and said, By what authority doest thou these things? and who gave thee this authority?

verses.greek: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Ἐρωτήσω ὑμᾶς κἀγὼ λόγον ἕνα, ὃν ἐὰν εἴπητέ μοι κἀγὼ ὑμῖν ἐρῶ ἐν ποίᾳ ἐξουσίᾳ ταῦτα ποιῶ·
verses.mistral: He replied to them, "I will also ask you one question, if you tell me the answer, I will tell you by what authority I do these things."
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto them, I also will ask you one thing, which if ye tell me, I in like wise will tell you by what authority I do these things.

verses.kjv: The baptism of John, whence was it? from heaven, or of men? And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say unto us, Why did ye not then believe him?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.mistral: Listen to another parable: A landowner planted a vineyard, enclosed it with a wall, dug a winepress in it, built a tower, and rented it to tenants, and went abroad.

verses.mistral: The farmers, seeing the son, said among themselves, "This is the heir; let us kill him and seize his inheritance."
verses.kjv: But when the husbandmen saw the son, they said among themselves, This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and let us seize on his inheritance.

verses.kjv: When the lord therefore of the vineyard cometh, what will he do unto those husbandmen?

verses.kjv: They say unto him, He will miserably destroy those wicked men, and will let out his vineyard unto other husbandmen, which shall render him the fruits in their seasons.

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto them, Did ye never read in the scriptures, The stone which the builders rejected, the same is become the head of the corner: this is the Lord's doing, and it is marvellous in our eyes?

verses.greek: ⸂Καὶ ὁ πεσὼν ἐπὶ τὸν λίθον τοῦτον συνθλασθήσεται· ἐφ’ ὃν δ’ ἂν πέσῃ λικμήσει αὐτόν.⸃

verses.greek: Καὶ ἀκούσαντες οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ Φαρισαῖοι τὰς παραβολὰς αὐτοῦ ἔγνωσαν ὅτι περὶ αὐτῶν λέγει·
verses.mistral: They heard the parables of Him, and the chief priests and Pharisees recognized that He was speaking about them
verses.kjv: And when the chief priests and Pharisees had heard his parables, they perceived that he spake of them.

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize

verses.mistral: Held in awe by the crowds, they sought to seize Him; for they had him as a prophet.
verses.kjv: But when they sought to lay hands on him, they feared the multitude, because they took him for a prophet.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ Ἰησοῦς πάλιν εἶπεν ⸂ἐν παραβολαῖς αὐτοῖς⸃ λέγων·
verses.mistral: And Jesus again speaking to them in parables said,
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and spake unto them again by parables, and said,

words.gloss: again, further, on the other hand

verses.greek: Ὡμοιώθη ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν ἀνθρώπῳ βασιλεῖ, ὅστις ἐποίησεν γάμους τῷ υἱῷ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: The kingdom of heaven is likened to a man who married his own son.
verses.kjv: The kingdom of heaven is like unto a certain king, which made a marriage for his son,

verses.greek: καὶ ἀπέστειλεν τοὺς δούλους αὐτοῦ καλέσαι τοὺς κεκλημένους εἰς τοὺς γάμους, καὶ οὐκ ἤθελον ἐλθεῖν.
verses.mistral: Called his servants to summon the invited guests for the wedding feast, but they would not come.
verses.kjv: And sent forth his servants to call them that were bidden to the wedding: and they would not come.

words.gloss: I send forth, send, send away, dismiss

verses.greek: πάλιν ἀπέστειλεν ἄλλους δούλους λέγων· Εἴπατε τοῖς κεκλημένοις· Ἰδοὺ τὸ ἄριστόν μου ⸀ἡτοίμακα, οἱ ταῦροί μου καὶ τὰ σιτιστὰ τεθυμένα, καὶ πάντα ἕτοιμα· δεῦτε εἰς τοὺς γάμους.
verses.mistral: Send forth more servants, saying: 'Behold, my preparation is ready, the oxen and the fatted cattle are killed, everything is set. Come to the marriage.'
verses.kjv: Again, he sent forth other servants, saying, Tell them which are bidden, Behold, I have prepared my dinner: my oxen and my fatlings are killed, and all things are ready: come unto the marriage.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: τότε ἐάν τις ὑμῖν εἴπῃ· Ἰδοὺ ὧδε ὁ χριστός, ἤ· Ὧδε, μὴ πιστεύσητε·
verses.mistral: Then if anyone says to you, "Here is the Christ," or, "Here," do not believe
verses.kjv: Then if any man shall say unto you, Lo, here is Christ, or there; believe it not.

verses.greek: ἐγερθήσονται γὰρ ψευδόχριστοι καὶ ψευδοπροφῆται, καὶ δώσουσιν σημεῖα μεγάλα καὶ τέρατα ὥστε ⸀πλανῆσαι εἰ δυνατὸν καὶ τοὺς ἐκλεκτούς·
verses.mistral: False Christs and false prophets will arise, and they will perform great signs and wonders to lead astray, if possible, even the elect.
verses.kjv: For there shall arise false Christs, and false prophets, and shall shew great signs and wonders; insomuch that, if it were possible, they shall deceive the very elect.

verses.greek: ἰδοὺ προείρηκα ὑμῖν.
verses.mistral: Here you are: "Indeed I have told you."
verses.kjv: Behold, I have told you before.

verses.greek: ἐὰν οὖν εἴπωσιν ὑμῖν· Ἰδοὺ ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ ἐστίν, μὴ ἐξέλθητε· Ἰδοὺ ἐν τοῖς ταμείοις, μὴ πιστεύσητε·
verses.mistral: Don't go out saying, "Here in the wilderness is the place," nor follow what says, "In the buildings is it."
verses.kjv: Wherefore if they shall say unto you, Behold, he is in the desert; go not forth: behold, he is in the secret chambers; believe it not.

verses.greek: ὥσπερ γὰρ ἡ ἀστραπὴ ἐξέρχεται ἀπὸ ἀνατολῶν καὶ φαίνεται ἕως δυσμῶν, οὕτως ⸀ἔσται ἡ παρουσία τοῦ υἱοῦ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου·
verses.mistral: The appearance of the Son of Man will be like the lightning flashing from east to west, and in the same way, it will be at the Kingdom of God.
verses.kjv: For as the lightning cometh out of the east, and shineth even unto the west; so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.

verses.greek: ⸀ὅπου ἐὰν ᾖ τὸ πτῶμα, ἐκεῖ συναχθήσονται οἱ ἀετοί.
verses.mistral: There where the carcass is, there the vultures will gather.
verses.kjv: For wheresoever the carcase is, there will the eagles be gathered together.

verses.greek: Εὐθέως δὲ μετὰ τὴν θλῖψιν τῶν ἡμερῶν ἐκείνων ὁ ἥλιος σκοτισθήσεται, καὶ ἡ σελήνη οὐ δώσει τὸ φέγγος αὐτῆς, καὶ οἱ ἀστέρες πεσοῦνται ἀπὸ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ, καὶ αἱ δυνάμεις τῶν οὐρανῶν σαλευθήσονται.
verses.mistral: Immediately after the distress of those days, the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light, the stars will fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
verses.kjv: ¶ Immediately after the tribulation of those days shall the sun be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light, and the stars shall fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens shall be shaken:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ τότε φανήσεται τὸ σημεῖον τοῦ υἱοῦ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐν ⸀τῷ οὐρανῷ, καὶ τότε κόψονται πᾶσαι αἱ φυλαὶ τῆς γῆς καὶ ὄψονται τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐρχόμενον ἐπὶ τῶν νεφελῶν τοῦ οὐρανοῦ μετὰ δυνάμεως καὶ δόξης πολλῆς·
verses.mistral: Then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in the sky, and then all tribes of the earth will mourn, and they will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of the sky with great power and glory.
verses.kjv: And then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven: and then shall all the tribes of the earth mourn, and they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: καὶ ἀποστελεῖ τοὺς ἀγγέλους αὐτοῦ μετὰ ⸀σάλπιγγος μεγάλης, καὶ ἐπισυνάξουσιν τοὺς ἐκλεκτοὺς αὐτοῦ ἐκ τῶν τεσσάρων ἀνέμων ἀπ’ ἄκρων οὐρανῶν ἕως ⸀τῶν ἄκρων αὐτῶν.
verses.mistral: And he will send out his angels with a great trumpet call, and they will gather together his elect from the four winds, from the ends of heaven to the ends of it.
verses.kjv: And he shall send his angels with a great sound of a trumpet, and they shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.

words.gloss: I send forth, send, send away, dismiss

verses.greek: Ἀπὸ δὲ τῆς συκῆς μάθετε τὴν παραβολήν· ὅταν ἤδη ὁ κλάδος αὐτῆς γένηται ἁπαλὸς καὶ τὰ φύλλα ἐκφύῃ, γινώσκετε ὅτι ἐγγὺς τὸ θέρος·
verses.mistral: From the fig tree learn its lesson: When its branch has become tender and its leaves have come out, you know that summer is near.
verses.kjv: Now learn a parable of the fig tree; When his branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is nigh:

verses.greek: οὕτως καὶ ὑμεῖς, ὅταν ἴδητε ⸂πάντα ταῦτα⸃, γινώσκετε ὅτι ἐγγύς ἐστιν ἐπὶ θύραις.
verses.mistral: Thus you also, when you see all these things, know that it is near at the doors.
verses.kjv: So likewise ye, when ye shall see all these things, know that it is near, even at the doors.

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ⸀ὅτι οὐ μὴ παρέλθῃ ἡ γενεὰ αὕτη ἕως ἂν πάντα ταῦτα γένηται.
verses.mistral: Verily I say unto you, this generation shall not pass away until all these things be fulfilled.
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass, till all these things be fulfilled.

words.gloss: verily, truly, amen, so let it be

verses.greek: ὁ οὐρανὸς καὶ ἡ γῆ ⸀παρελεύσεται, οἱ δὲ λόγοι μου οὐ μὴ παρέλθωσιν.
verses.mistral: The heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.
verses.kjv: Heaven and earth shall pass away, but my words shall not pass away.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Περὶ δὲ τῆς ἡμέρας ἐκείνης καὶ ὥρας οὐδεὶς οἶδεν, οὐδὲ οἱ ἄγγελοι τῶν οὐρανῶν ⸂οὐδὲ ὁ υἱός⸃, εἰ μὴ ὁ ⸀πατὴρ μόνος.
verses.mistral: That hour and that day no one knows, neither the angels of heaven nor the Son, except the Father alone.
verses.kjv: ¶ But of that day and hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels of heaven, but my Father only.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὥσπερ ⸀γὰρ αἱ ἡμέραι τοῦ Νῶε, οὕτως ⸀ἔσται ἡ παρουσία τοῦ υἱοῦ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου·
verses.mistral: Like the days of Noah, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
verses.kjv: But as the days of Noe were, so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.

verses.greek: ⸀ὡς γὰρ ἦσαν ἐν ταῖς ⸀ἡμέραις ταῖς πρὸ τοῦ κατακλυσμοῦ τρώγοντες καὶ πίνοντες, γαμοῦντες καὶ ⸀γαμίζοντες, ἄχρι ἧς ἡμέρας εἰσῆλθεν Νῶε εἰς τὴν κιβωτόν,
verses.mistral: As in the days before the flood, they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that Noah entered the ark.
verses.kjv: For as in the days that were before the flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that Noe entered into the ark,

words.gloss: as far as, up to, until
I go in, come in, enter

verses.greek: καὶ οὐκ ἔγνωσαν ἕως ἦλθεν ὁ κατακλυσμὸς καὶ ἦρεν ἅπαντας, οὕτως ἔσται ⸀καὶ ἡ παρουσία τοῦ υἱοῦ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου.
verses.mistral: And they did not know until the flood came and took them all away. So it will be with the coming of the Son of Man.
verses.kjv: And knew not until the flood came, and took them all away; so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize

verses.greek: τότε ⸂δύο ἔσονται⸃ ἐν τῷ ἀγρῷ, ⸀εἷς παραλαμβάνεται καὶ ⸁εἷς ἀφίεται·
verses.mistral: Then two will be in the field; one is taken and one is left.
verses.kjv: Then shall two be in the field; the one shall be taken, and the other left.

words.gloss: I send away, release, remit, forgive, permit

verses.greek: δύο ἀλήθουσαι ἐν τῷ ⸀μύλῳ, μία παραλαμβάνεται καὶ μία ἀφίεται.
verses.mistral: Two will be grinding in the mill; one is taken and one is left.
verses.kjv: Two women shall be grinding at the mill; the one shall be taken, and the other left.

words.gloss: I send away, release, remit, forgive, permit

verses.greek: γρηγορεῖτε οὖν, ὅτι οὐκ οἴδατε ποίᾳ ⸀ἡμέρᾳ ὁ κύριος ὑμῶν ἔρχεται.
verses.mistral: Watch therefore, for you do not know on what day your Lord is coming.
verses.kjv: ¶ Watch therefore: for ye know not what hour your Lord doth come.

words.gloss: I am awake, am vigilant, watch

verses.greek: ἐκεῖνο δὲ γινώσκετε ὅτι εἰ ᾔδει ὁ οἰκοδεσπότης ποίᾳ φυλακῇ ὁ κλέπτης ἔρχεται, ἐγρηγόρησεν ἂν καὶ οὐκ ἂν εἴασεν διορυχθῆναι τὴν οἰκίαν αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: That man, however, knows that if the master of the house had known what watch he was coming on, he would have been awake and would not have allowed his house to be broken into.
verses.kjv: But know this, that if the goodman of the house had known in what watch the thief would come, he would have watched, and would not have suffered his house to be broken up.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come to know, learn, realize
I am awake, am vigilant, watch

verses.greek: διὰ τοῦτο καὶ ὑμεῖς γίνεσθε ἕτοιμοι, ὅτι ᾗ ⸂οὐ δοκεῖτε ὥρᾳ⸃ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἔρχεται.
verses.mistral: Therefore, you also be ready, for at an hour that you do not expect, the Son of Man is coming.
verses.kjv: Therefore be ye also ready: for in such an hour as ye think not the Son of man cometh.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born
I think, seem, appear, it seems

verses.greek: Τίς ἄρα ἐστὶν ὁ πιστὸς δοῦλος καὶ φρόνιμος ὃν κατέστησεν ὁ ⸀κύριος ἐπὶ τῆς ⸀οἰκετείας αὐτοῦ τοῦ ⸀δοῦναι αὐτοῖς τὴν τροφὴν ἐν καιρῷ;
verses.mistral: Who then is the faithful and wise servant, whom his master has set over his household, to give them food at the right time?
verses.kjv: Who then is a faithful and wise servant, whom his lord hath made ruler over his household, to give them meat in due season?

words.gloss: a particle asking a question, expecting a negative response
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: μακάριος ὁ δοῦλος ἐκεῖνος ὃν ἐλθὼν ὁ κύριος αὐτοῦ εὑρήσει ⸂οὕτως ποιοῦντα⸃·
verses.mistral: Blessed is that servant whom, upon his master's arrival, his master finds so doing.
verses.kjv: Blessed is that servant, whom his lord when he cometh shall find so doing.

verses.greek: ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι ἐπὶ πᾶσιν τοῖς ὑπάρχουσιν αὐτοῦ καταστήσει αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: All his possessions he will make him ruler over them.
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, That he shall make him ruler over all his goods.

words.gloss: verily, truly, amen, so let it be
on, to, against, on the basis of, at
I am, exist, am in possession

verses.greek: ἐὰν δὲ εἴπῃ ὁ κακὸς δοῦλος ἐκεῖνος ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ αὐτοῦ· Χρονίζει ⸂μου ὁ κύριος⸃,
verses.mistral: Delay is my will, the Lord.
verses.kjv: But and if that evil servant shall say in his heart, My lord delayeth his coming;

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ ἄρξηται τύπτειν τοὺς συνδούλους ⸂αὐτοῦ, ἐσθίῃ δὲ καὶ πίνῃ⸃ μετὰ τῶν μεθυόντων,
verses.mistral: Begins to strike his servants, and eats and drinks with the drunkards
verses.kjv: And shall begin to smite his fellowservants, and to eat and drink with the drunken;

words.gloss: I beat, strike, wound, inflict punishment
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἥξει ὁ κύριος τοῦ δούλου ἐκείνου ἐν ἡμέρᾳ ᾗ οὐ προσδοκᾷ καὶ ἐν ὥρᾳ ᾗ οὐ γινώσκει,
verses.mistral: Will come the Lord of that servant in a day when he does not expect, and at an hour when he does not know.
verses.kjv: The lord of that servant shall come in a day when he looketh not for him, and in an hour that he is not aware of,

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize

verses.greek: καὶ διχοτομήσει αὐτὸν καὶ τὸ μέρος αὐτοῦ μετὰ τῶν ὑποκριτῶν θήσει· ἐκεῖ ἔσται ὁ κλαυθμὸς καὶ ὁ βρυγμὸς τῶν ὀδόντων.
verses.mistral: And he will divide him, and his part with the hypocrites; there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
verses.kjv: And shall cut him asunder, and appoint him his portion with the hypocrites: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

words.gloss: I cut in two, scourge severely

verses.greek: Τότε ὁμοιωθήσεται ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν δέκα παρθένοις, αἵτινες λαβοῦσαι τὰς λαμπάδας ⸀ἑαυτῶν ἐξῆλθον εἰς ⸀ὑπάντησιν τοῦ νυμφίου.
verses.mistral: Then the kingdom of heaven shall be likened to ten virgins, who took their lamps and went out to meet the bridegroom.
verses.kjv: Then shall the kingdom of heaven be likened unto ten virgins, which took their lamps, and went forth to meet the bridegroom.

verses.greek: πέντε δὲ ⸂ἐξ αὐτῶν ἦσαν⸃ ⸂μωραὶ καὶ πέντε φρόνιμοι⸃.
verses.mistral: Five were therefore wise and five were foolish.
verses.kjv: And five of them were wise, and five were foolish.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ⸂αἱ γὰρ⸃ μωραὶ λαβοῦσαι τὰς λαμπάδας αὐτῶν οὐκ ἔλαβον μεθ’ ἑαυτῶν ἔλαιον·
verses.mistral: Foolish virgins took no oil with them.
verses.kjv: They that were foolish took their lamps, and took no oil with them:

verses.greek: αἱ δὲ φρόνιμοι ἔλαβον ἔλαιον ἐν τοῖς ⸀ἀγγείοις μετὰ τῶν λαμπάδων ⸀ἑαυτῶν.
verses.mistral: Wise virgins took oil in their vessels with their lamps.
verses.kjv: But the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: χρονίζοντος δὲ τοῦ νυμφίου ἐνύσταξαν πᾶσαι καὶ ἐκάθευδον.
verses.mistral: The bridegroom was delayed, and they all became drowsy and fell asleep.
verses.kjv: While the bridegroom tarried, they all slumbered and slept.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I nod in sleep, am drowsy, slumber

verses.greek: μέσης δὲ νυκτὸς κραυγὴ γέγονεν· Ἰδοὺ ὁ ⸀νυμφίος, ἐξέρχεσθε εἰς ἀπάντησιν ⸀αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: In the middle of the night there was a cry. Behold, the bridegroom is coming; go out to meet him.
verses.kjv: And at midnight there was a cry made, Behold, the bridegroom cometh; go ye out to meet him.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: τότε ἠγέρθησαν πᾶσαι αἱ παρθένοι ἐκεῖναι καὶ ἐκόσμησαν τὰς λαμπάδας ⸀ἑαυτῶν.
verses.mistral: Then all the virgins there arose and trimmed their lamps.
verses.kjv: Then all those virgins arose, and trimmed their lamps.

verses.greek: αἱ δὲ μωραὶ ταῖς φρονίμοις εἶπαν· Δότε ἡμῖν ἐκ τοῦ ἐλαίου ὑμῶν, ὅτι αἱ λαμπάδες ἡμῶν σβέννυνται.
verses.mistral: The foolish said to the wise, "Give us some of your oil, for our lamps are going out."
verses.kjv: And the foolish said unto the wise, Give us of your oil; for our lamps are gone out.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἀπεκρίθησαν δὲ αἱ φρόνιμοι λέγουσαι· Μήποτε ⸂οὐ μὴ⸃ ἀρκέσῃ ἡμῖν καὶ ὑμῖν· ⸀πορεύεσθε μᾶλλον πρὸς τοὺς πωλοῦντας καὶ ἀγοράσατε ἑαυταῖς.
verses.mistral: The wise replied, "Perhaps it will be sufficient for us and for you. Go rather to those who sell and buy for yourselves."
verses.kjv: But the wise answered, saying, Not so; lest there be not enough for us and you: but go ye rather to them that sell, and buy for yourselves.

lest at any time, lest, perhaps
I am sufficient, I suffice, am content, satisfied

verses.greek: ἀπερχομένων δὲ αὐτῶν ἀγοράσαι ἦλθεν ὁ νυμφίος, καὶ αἱ ἕτοιμοι εἰσῆλθον μετ’ αὐτοῦ εἰς τοὺς γάμους, καὶ ἐκλείσθη ἡ θύρα.
verses.mistral: The groom went out to buy, and those who were ready went in with him to the wedding feast, and the door was locked.
verses.kjv: And while they went to buy, the bridegroom came; and they that were ready went in with him to the marriage: and the door was shut.

but, on the other hand, and
I go in, come in, enter

verses.greek: ὕστερον δὲ ἔρχονται καὶ αἱ λοιπαὶ παρθένοι λέγουσαι· Κύριε κύριε, ἄνοιξον ἡμῖν·
verses.mistral: Later come also the remaining virgins and saying: Lord, Lord, open to us.
verses.kjv: Afterward came also the other virgins, saying, Lord, Lord, open to us.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν· Ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν, οὐκ οἶδα ὑμᾶς.
verses.mistral: He replied and said, "Truly I tell you, I do not know you."
verses.kjv: But he answered and said, Verily I say unto you, I know you not.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
verily, truly, amen, so let it be

verses.greek: γρηγορεῖτε οὖν, ὅτι οὐκ οἴδατε τὴν ἡμέραν οὐδὲ τὴν ⸀ὥραν.
verses.mistral: Therefore be alert, for you do not know the day or the hour.
verses.kjv: Watch therefore, for ye know neither the day nor the hour wherein the Son of man cometh.

words.gloss: I am awake, am vigilant, watch

verses.greek: Ὥσπερ γὰρ ἄνθρωπος ἀποδημῶν ἐκάλεσεν τοὺς ἰδίους δούλους καὶ παρέδωκεν αὐτοῖς τὰ ὑπάρχοντα αὐτοῦ,
verses.mistral: Similarly, when a man goes on a journey, he summons his own servants and entrusts his possessions to them.
verses.kjv: ¶ For the kingdom of heaven is as a man travelling into a far country, who called his own servants, and delivered unto them his goods.

words.gloss: I am, exist, am in possession

verses.greek: καὶ ᾧ μὲν ἔδωκεν πέντε τάλαντα ᾧ δὲ δύο ᾧ δὲ ἕν, ἑκάστῳ κατὰ τὴν ἰδίαν δύναμιν, καὶ ἀπεδήμησεν. εὐθέως
verses.mistral: Gave five talents to one, to another two, to another one, each according to his own ability, and went away.
verses.kjv: And unto one he gave five talents, to another two, and to another one; to every man according to his several ability; and straightway took his journey.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and
down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: ⸀πορευθεὶς ὁ τὰ πέντε τάλαντα λαβὼν ἠργάσατο ἐν αὐτοῖς καὶ ⸀ἐκέρδησεν ἄλλα ⸀πέντε·
verses.mistral: Earned five more in them
verses.kjv: Then he that had received the five talents went and traded with the same, and made them other five talents.

verses.greek: ⸀ὡσαύτως ὁ τὰ δύο ⸀ἐκέρδησεν ἄλλα δύο·
verses.mistral: Earned also the one two more two. (This translation is a literal rendition of the Greek text, but it may not flow smoothly in English as it's not meant to be read this way. A more idiomatic and grammatically correct translation would be: "He likewise earned five more for the two.")
verses.kjv: And likewise he that had received two, he also gained other two.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ τὸ ἓν λαβὼν ἀπελθὼν ὤρυξεν ⸀γῆν καὶ ⸀ἔκρυψεν τὸ ἀργύριον τοῦ κυρίου αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: He took the one and going, buried land and hid his lord's silver.
verses.kjv: But he that had received one went and digged in the earth, and hid his lord's money.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come or go away from, depart, return

verses.greek: μετὰ δὲ ⸂πολὺν χρόνον⸃ ἔρχεται ὁ κύριος τῶν δούλων ἐκείνων καὶ συναίρει ⸂λόγον μετ’ αὐτῶν⸃
verses.mistral: Master comes after a long time, confronts them regarding the silver.
verses.kjv: After a long time the lord of those servants cometh, and reckoneth with them.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I make a reckoning, settle accounts

verses.greek: καὶ προσελθὼν ὁ τὰ πέντε τάλαντα λαβὼν προσήνεγκεν ἄλλα πέντε τάλαντα λέγων· Κύριε, πέντε τάλαντά μοι παρέδωκας· ἴδε ἄλλα πέντε τάλαντα ⸀ἐκέρδησα.
verses.mistral: And coming, the one of the five talents took and brought five more talents, saying, "Master, five talents I have given you; see, five more talents I have made."
verses.kjv: And so he that had received five talents came and brought other five talents, saying, Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents: behold, I have gained beside them five talents more.

verses.greek: ⸀ἔφη αὐτῷ ὁ κύριος αὐτοῦ· Εὖ, δοῦλε ἀγαθὲ καὶ πιστέ, ἐπὶ ὀλίγα ἦς πιστός, ἐπὶ πολλῶν σε καταστήσω· εἴσελθε εἰς τὴν χαρὰν τοῦ κυρίου σου.
verses.mistral: The Lord said to him, "Well done, good and faithful servant. You have been trustworthy with a little; I will put you in charge of much. Come into the joy of your master."
verses.kjv: His lord said unto him, Well done, thou good and faithful servant: thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will make thee ruler over many things: enter thou into the joy of thy lord.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.mistral: Returning the two talents, he said, "Lord, you gave me two talents; see, I have gained two more."

verses.mistral: The Lord said to him, "Well done, good and faithful servant. You were faithful with a few things, I will put you in charge of many things; come and share in the joy of your master."

verses.mistral: You ought therefore to have given my money to the bankers, and on coming I would have received back my own with interest.

verses.kjv: Take therefore the talent from him, and give it unto him which hath ten talents.

verses.greek: τῷ γὰρ ἔχοντι παντὶ δοθήσεται καὶ περισσευθήσεται· ⸂τοῦ δὲ⸃ μὴ ἔχοντος καὶ ὃ ἔχει ἀρθήσεται ἀπ’ αὐτοῦ.

verses.mistral: Then the Son of Man will come in His glory, and all the angels with Him. Then He will sit on the throne of His glory.

on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.mistral: And all nations will be gathered before Him, and He will separate them one from another, just as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.

verses.kjv: And he shall set the sheep on his right hand, but the goats on the left.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: τότε ἐρεῖ ὁ βασιλεὺς τοῖς ἐκ δεξιῶν αὐτοῦ· Δεῦτε, οἱ εὐλογημένοι τοῦ πατρός μου, κληρονομήσατε τὴν ἡτοιμασμένην ὑμῖν βασιλείαν ἀπὸ καταβολῆς κόσμου.
verses.mistral: Then the King will say to those on His right, "Come, you who are blessed by My Father; inherit the kingdom prepared for you since the creation of the world."
verses.kjv: Then shall the King say unto them on his right hand, Come, ye blessed of my Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world:

verses.greek: ἐπείνασα γὰρ καὶ ἐδώκατέ μοι φαγεῖν, ἐδίψησα καὶ ἐποτίσατέ με, ξένος ἤμην καὶ συνηγάγετέ με,
verses.mistral: I was hungry and you gave me food, I was thirsty and you gave me drink, a stranger and you took me in.

words.gloss: I look upon, visit, look out, select

verses.greek: τότε ἀποκριθήσονται αὐτῷ οἱ δίκαιοι λέγοντες· Κύριε, πότε σε εἴδομεν πεινῶντα καὶ ἐθρέψαμεν, ἢ διψῶντα καὶ ἐποτίσαμεν;
verses.mistral: Then the righteous will answer him, saying, "Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you drink?"

words.gloss: I cause to drink, give to drink

verses.greek: πότε δέ σε εἴδομεν ξένον καὶ συνηγάγομεν, ἢ γυμνὸν καὶ περιεβάλομεν;

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.kjv: Then shall they also answer him, saying, Lord, when saw we thee an hungred, or athirst, or a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, and did not minister unto thee?

verses.greek: τότε ἀποκριθήσεται αὐτοῖς λέγων· Ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν, ἐφ’ ὅσον οὐκ ἐποιήσατε ἑνὶ τούτων τῶν ἐλαχίστων, οὐδὲ ἐμοὶ ἐποιήσατε.
verses.mistral: Then He will answer them, saying, "Truly I tell you, whatever you did not do for one of the least of these, you did not do for me."

verses.mistral: Witnessing this, the disciples became indignant, saying, "To what purpose is this waste?"
verses.kjv: But when his disciples saw it, they had indignation, saying, To what purpose is this waste?

verses.mistral: Realizing this, Jesus said to them, "Why do you trouble the woman? For she has done a good work for me."

verses.mistral: Then one of the twelve, called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests

words.gloss: verily, truly, amen, so let it be

verses.mistral: And deeply saddened, they began to say to Him: "Is it I, Lord?"
verses.kjv: And they were exceeding sorrowful, and began every one of them to say unto him, Lord, is it I?

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν· Ὁ ἐμβάψας μετ’ ἐμοῦ ⸂τὴν χεῖρα ἐν τῷ τρυβλίῳ⸃ οὗτός με παραδώσει·
verses.mistral: The one who has betrayed Me with the kiss in the dish will betray Me.

verses.greek: ὁ μὲν υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ὑπάγει καθὼς γέγραπται περὶ αὐτοῦ, οὐαὶ δὲ τῷ ἀνθρώπῳ ἐκείνῳ δι’ οὗ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου παραδίδοται· καλὸν ἦν αὐτῷ εἰ οὐκ ἐγεννήθη ὁ ἄνθρωπος ἐκεῖνος.

verses.mistral: Judas, who betrayed Him, replied, "Surely I am not He, Rabbi?" He said to him, "You have said."

I shout upwards, cry out, raise my voice

verses.greek: τινὲς δὲ τῶν ἐκεῖ ⸀ἑστηκότων ἀκούσαντες ἔλεγον ὅτι Ἠλίαν φωνεῖ οὗτος.
verses.mistral: This one among those standing there heard that it is Elijah who is calling.
verses.kjv: Some of them that stood there, when they heard that, said, This man calleth for Elias.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ εὐθέως δραμὼν εἷς ἐξ αὐτῶν καὶ λαβὼν σπόγγον πλήσας τε ὄξους καὶ περιθεὶς καλάμῳ ἐπότιζεν αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: And straightway one of them ran, and taking a sponge filled it with vinegar, and putting it on a reed, gave him to drink.
verses.kjv: And straightway one of them ran, and took a spunge, and filled it with vinegar, and put it on a reed, and gave him to drink.

words.gloss: I cause to drink, give to drink

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ λοιποὶ ⸀ἔλεγον· Ἄφες ἴδωμεν εἰ ἔρχεται Ἠλίας σώσων ⸀αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: They said, "Let him be; let us see if Elijah comes to save him."
verses.kjv: The rest said, Let be, let us see whether Elias will come to save him.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I send away, release, remit, forgive, permit

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς πάλιν κράξας φωνῇ μεγάλῃ ἀφῆκεν τὸ πνεῦμα.
verses.mistral: Jesus again cried out with a loud voice and gave up his spirit.
verses.kjv: ¶ Jesus, when he had cried again with a loud voice, yielded up the ghost.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
again, further, on the other hand
I send away, release, remit, forgive, permit

verses.greek: καὶ ἰδοὺ τὸ καταπέτασμα τοῦ ναοῦ ἐσχίσθη ⸂ἀπ’ ἄνωθεν ἕως κάτω εἰς δύο⸃, καὶ ἡ γῆ ἐσείσθη, καὶ αἱ πέτραι ἐσχίσθησαν,
verses.mistral: The veil of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom, and the earth shook, and the rocks were split.
verses.kjv: And, behold, the veil of the temple was rent in twain from the top to the bottom; and the earth did quake, and the rocks rent;

words.gloss: from above, from the beginning, again

verses.greek: καὶ τὰ μνημεῖα ἀνεῴχθησαν καὶ πολλὰ σώματα τῶν κεκοιμημένων ἁγίων ⸀ἠγέρθησαν,
verses.mistral: And the tombs were opened, and many bodies of the saints who had fallen asleep were raised.
verses.kjv: And the graves were opened; and many bodies of the saints which slept arose,

verses.greek: καὶ ἐξελθόντες ἐκ τῶν μνημείων μετὰ τὴν ἔγερσιν αὐτοῦ εἰσῆλθον εἰς τὴν ἁγίαν πόλιν καὶ ἐνεφανίσθησαν πολλοῖς.
verses.mistral: Emerged from the tombs after his resurrection, they entered the holy city and appeared to many.
verses.kjv: And came out of the graves after his resurrection, and went into the holy city, and appeared unto many.

words.gloss: I go in, come in, enter
I make visible, report against, appear before

verses.greek: Ὁ δὲ ἑκατόνταρχος καὶ οἱ μετ’ αὐτοῦ τηροῦντες τὸν Ἰησοῦν ἰδόντες τὸν σεισμὸν καὶ τὰ ⸀γενόμενα ἐφοβήθησαν σφόδρα λέγοντες· Ἀληθῶς ⸂θεοῦ υἱὸς⸃ ἦν οὗτος.
verses.mistral: This tomb-guarding centurion and those with him, who had been guarding Jesus, seeing the earthquake and what had happened, were terrified incredibly, saying, "Truly, this was the Son of God."
verses.kjv: Now when the centurion, and they that were with him, watching Jesus, saw the earthquake, and those things that were done, they feared greatly, saying, Truly this was the Son of God.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: Ἦσαν δὲ ἐκεῖ γυναῖκες πολλαὶ ἀπὸ μακρόθεν θεωροῦσαι, αἵτινες ἠκολούθησαν τῷ Ἰησοῦ ἀπὸ τῆς Γαλιλαίας διακονοῦσαι αὐτῷ·
verses.mistral: Several women from afar were watching, who had followed Jesus from Galilee and ministered to Him.
verses.kjv: And many women were there beholding afar off, which followed Jesus from Galilee, ministering unto him:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἐν αἷς ἦν Μαρία ἡ Μαγδαληνὴ καὶ Μαρία ἡ τοῦ Ἰακώβου καὶ ⸀Ἰωσὴφ μήτηρ καὶ ἡ μήτηρ τῶν υἱῶν Ζεβεδαίου.
verses.mistral: In these were Mary Magdalene, and Mary the wife of James, and Joseph's mother, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.
verses.kjv: Among which was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and the mother of Zebedee's children.

verses.greek: Ὀψίας δὲ γενομένης ἦλθεν ἄνθρωπος πλούσιος ἀπὸ Ἁριμαθαίας, τοὔνομα Ἰωσήφ, ὃς καὶ αὐτὸς ⸀ἐμαθητεύθη τῷ Ἰησοῦ·
verses.mistral: Evening came, and a rich man from Harimathais named Joseph, who was also a disciple of Jesus, arrived
verses.kjv: When the even was come, there came a rich man of Arimathaea, named Joseph, who also himself was Jesus' disciple:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come into being, am born
I make disciples, make into disciples

verses.greek: οὗτος προσελθὼν τῷ Πιλάτῳ ᾐτήσατο τὸ σῶμα τοῦ Ἰησοῦ. τότε ὁ Πιλᾶτος ἐκέλευσεν ⸀ἀποδοθῆναι.
verses.mistral: This man approaching Pilate asked for the body of Jesus. Then Pilate commanded it to be given.
verses.kjv: He went to Pilate, and begged the body of Jesus. Then Pilate commanded the body to be delivered.

verses.greek: καὶ λαβὼν τὸ σῶμα ὁ Ἰωσὴφ ἐνετύλιξεν ⸀αὐτὸ σινδόνι καθαρᾷ,
verses.mistral: And Joseph taking the body, he wrapped it in a clean linen shroud.
verses.kjv: And when Joseph had taken the body, he wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,

words.gloss: I wrap up, roll round, envelop

verses.greek: καὶ ἔθηκεν αὐτὸ ἐν τῷ καινῷ αὐτοῦ μνημείῳ ὃ ἐλατόμησεν ἐν τῇ πέτρᾳ, καὶ προσκυλίσας λίθον μέγαν τῇ θύρᾳ τοῦ μνημείου ἀπῆλθεν.
verses.mistral: Placed him in his new tomb, which he had hewn out in the rock, and after rolling a large stone to the door of the tomb, he went away.
verses.kjv: And laid it in his own new tomb, which he had hewn out in the rock: and he rolled a great stone to the door of the sepulchre, and departed.

words.gloss: I come or go away from, depart, return

verses.greek: ἦν δὲ ἐκεῖ ⸀Μαριὰμ ἡ Μαγδαληνὴ καὶ ἡ ἄλλη Μαρία καθήμεναι ἀπέναντι τοῦ τάφου.
verses.mistral: They were there in front of the tomb, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary.
verses.kjv: And there was Mary Magdalene, and the other Mary, sitting over against the sepulchre.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
over against, opposite, in view of, in the presence of

verses.greek: Τῇ δὲ ἐπαύριον, ἥτις ἐστὶν μετὰ τὴν παρασκευήν, συνήχθησαν οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ Φαρισαῖοι πρὸς Πιλᾶτον
verses.mistral: The next day, which was after the Sabbath, the chief priests and Pharisees assembled to Pilate
verses.kjv: ¶ Now the next day, that followed the day of the preparation, the chief priests and Pharisees came together unto Pilate,

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: λέγοντες· Κύριε, ἐμνήσθημεν ὅτι ἐκεῖνος ὁ πλάνος εἶπεν ἔτι ζῶν· Μετὰ τρεῖς ἡμέρας ἐγείρομαι·
verses.mistral: Reminded, they said to Him: "Sir, we remember that this deceiver said while still alive: 'After three days I will rise.'"
verses.kjv: Saying, Sir, we remember that that deceiver said, while he was yet alive, After three days I will rise again.

verses.greek: κέλευσον οὖν ἀσφαλισθῆναι τὸν τάφον ἕως τῆς τρίτης ἡμέρας, μήποτε ἐλθόντες οἱ μαθηταὶ ⸀αὐτοῦ κλέψωσιν αὐτὸν καὶ εἴπωσιν τῷ λαῷ· Ἠγέρθη ἀπὸ τῶν νεκρῶν, καὶ ἔσται ἡ ἐσχάτη πλάνη χείρων τῆς πρώτης.
verses.mistral: Secure the tomb until the third day, lest his disciples come and steal him away and say to the people, 'He has risen from the dead,' and the last deception will be worse than the first.
verses.kjv: Command therefore that the sepulchre be made sure until the third day, lest his disciples come by night, and steal him away, and say unto the people, He is risen from the dead: so the last error shall be worse than the first.

words.gloss: lest at any time, lest, perhaps

verses.greek: ⸀ἔφη αὐτοῖς ὁ Πιλᾶτος· Ἔχετε κουστωδίαν· ὑπάγετε ἀσφαλίσασθε ὡς οἴδατε.
verses.mistral: Pilate said to them, "Take a guard; go and secure as you know."
verses.kjv: Pilate said unto them, Ye have a watch: go your way, make it as sure as ye can.

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ πορευθέντες ἠσφαλίσαντο τὸν τάφον σφραγίσαντες τὸν λίθον μετὰ τῆς κουστωδίας.
verses.mistral: They rolled away the stone, sealing the tomb with a slab.
verses.kjv: So they went, and made the sepulchre sure, sealing the stone, and setting a watch.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Ὀψὲ δὲ σαββάτων, τῇ ἐπιφωσκούσῃ εἰς μίαν σαββάτων, ἦλθεν ⸀Μαριὰμ ἡ Μαγδαληνὴ καὶ ἡ ἄλλη Μαρία θεωρῆσαι τὸν τάφον.
verses.mistral: Early on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to see the tomb.
verses.kjv: In the end of the sabbath, as it began to dawn toward the first day of the week, came Mary Magdalene and the other Mary to see the sepulchre.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ ἰδοὺ σεισμὸς ἐγένετο μέγας· ἄγγελος γὰρ κυρίου καταβὰς ἐξ οὐρανοῦ ⸀καὶ προσελθὼν ἀπεκύλισε τὸν ⸀λίθον καὶ ἐκάθητο ἐπάνω αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: And behold, there was a great earthquake; for an angel of the Lord descended from heaven, and came and rolled back the stone and sat on it.
verses.kjv: And, behold, there was a great earthquake: for the angel of the Lord descended from heaven, and came and rolled back the stone from the door, and sat upon it.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born
on the top, above, superior to

verses.greek: ἦν δὲ ἡ εἰδέα αὐτοῦ ⸀ὡς ἀστραπὴ καὶ τὸ ἔνδυμα αὐτοῦ λευκὸν ὡς χιών.
verses.mistral: His appearance was like lightning, and his robe white as snow.
verses.kjv: His countenance was like lightning, and his raiment white as snow:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἀπὸ δὲ τοῦ φόβου αὐτοῦ ἐσείσθησαν οἱ τηροῦντες καὶ ἐγενήθησαν ⸀ὡς νεκροί.
verses.mistral: Frightened, those keeping watch became like dead men.
verses.kjv: And for fear of him the keepers did shake, and became as dead men.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ ἄγγελος εἶπεν ταῖς γυναιξίν· Μὴ φοβεῖσθε ὑμεῖς, οἶδα γὰρ ὅτι Ἰησοῦν τὸν ἐσταυρωμένον ζητεῖτε·
verses.mistral: Fear not, for I know that you seek Jesus who was crucified.
verses.kjv: And the angel answered and said unto the women, Fear not ye: for I know that ye seek Jesus, which was crucified.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: οὐκ ἔστιν ὧδε, ἠγέρθη γὰρ καθὼς εἶπεν· δεῦτε ἴδετε τὸν τόπον ὅπου ⸀ἔκειτο·
verses.mistral: He is not here, for he has risen, as he said. Come, see the place where he was lying.
verses.kjv: He is not here: for he is risen, as he said. Come, see the place where the Lord lay.

verses.greek: καὶ ταχὺ πορευθεῖσαι εἴπατε τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ ὅτι Ἠγέρθη ἀπὸ τῶν νεκρῶν, καὶ ἰδοὺ προάγει ὑμᾶς εἰς τὴν Γαλιλαίαν, ἐκεῖ αὐτὸν ὄψεσθε· ἰδοὺ εἶπον ὑμῖν.
verses.mistral: He is risen from the dead; go tell his disciples. And behold, he goes before you into Galilee; there you will see him. Just as I told you.
verses.kjv: And go quickly, and tell his disciples that he is risen from the dead; and, behold, he goeth before you into Galilee; there shall ye see him: lo, I have told you.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀ἀπελθοῦσαι ταχὺ ἀπὸ τοῦ μνημείου μετὰ φόβου καὶ χαρᾶς μεγάλης ἔδραμον ἀπαγγεῖλαι τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: And fled quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy, to announce to his disciples.
verses.kjv: And they departed quickly from the sepulchre with fear and great joy; and did run to bring his disciples word.

words.gloss: I come or go away from, depart, return
I report, bring a report, announce

verses.greek: ⸀καὶ ⸀ἰδοὺ Ἰησοῦς ⸀ὑπήντησεν αὐταῖς λέγων· Χαίρετε· αἱ δὲ προσελθοῦσαι ἐκράτησαν αὐτοῦ τοὺς πόδας καὶ προσεκύνησαν αὐτῷ.
verses.mistral: Jesus appeared to them and said, "Rejoice!" Then the women approached and clasped his feet and worshiped him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And as they went to tell his disciples, behold, Jesus met them, saying, All hail. And they came and held him by the feet, and worshipped him.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: τότε λέγει αὐταῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς· Μὴ φοβεῖσθε· ὑπάγετε ἀπαγγείλατε τοῖς ἀδελφοῖς μου ἵνα ἀπέλθωσιν εἰς τὴν Γαλιλαίαν, κἀκεῖ με ὄψονται.
verses.mistral: Then Jesus says to them, "Fear not; go and tell my brothers to go into Galilee, there they will see me."
verses.kjv: Then said Jesus unto them, Be not afraid: go tell my brethren that they go into Galilee, and there shall they see me.

words.gloss: I report, bring a report, announce
I come or go away from, depart, return

verses.greek: Πορευομένων δὲ αὐτῶν ἰδού τινες τῆς κουστωδίας ἐλθόντες εἰς τὴν πόλιν ἀπήγγειλαν τοῖς ἀρχιερεῦσιν ἅπαντα τὰ γενόμενα.
verses.mistral: Upon their departure, some women from the group of Galileans went to the city and reported to the chief priests everything that had happened.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now when they were going, behold, some of the watch came into the city, and shewed unto the chief priests all the things that were done.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I report, bring a report, announce
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: καὶ συναχθέντες μετὰ τῶν πρεσβυτέρων συμβούλιόν τε λαβόντες ἀργύρια ἱκανὰ ἔδωκαν τοῖς στρατιώταις
verses.mistral: Gathering with the elders, they took counsel and paid sufficient silver to the soldiers (For verse 28:13, the Greek is: And going away, they dispersed among the tombs with the eleven disciples.)
verses.kjv: And when they were assembled with the elders, and had taken counsel, they gave large money unto the soldiers,

verses.greek: λέγοντες· Εἴπατε ὅτι Οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ νυκτὸς ἐλθόντες ἔκλεψαν αὐτὸν ἡμῶν κοιμωμένων·
verses.mistral: Said: "They have taken Him away while we were asleep."
verses.kjv: Saying, Say ye, His disciples came by night, and stole him away while we slept.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐὰν ἀκουσθῇ τοῦτο ⸀ἐπὶ τοῦ ἡγεμόνος, ἡμεῖς πείσομεν ⸀αὐτὸν καὶ ὑμᾶς ἀμερίμνους ποιήσομεν.
verses.mistral: And if this is heard by the ruler, we will persuade him and you will be made carefree.
verses.kjv: And if this come to the governor's ears, we will persuade him, and secure you.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ λαβόντες ⸀τὰ ἀργύρια ἐποίησαν ὡς ἐδιδάχθησαν. Καὶ διεφημίσθη ὁ λόγος οὗτος παρὰ Ἰουδαίοις μέχρι τῆς σήμερον ⸀ἡμέρας.
verses.mistral: The disciples, having taken the money, did as they were instructed. And this message has been circulated among Jews to this very day.
verses.kjv: So they took the money, and did as they were taught: and this saying is commonly reported among the Jews until this day.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Οἱ δὲ ἕνδεκα μαθηταὶ ἐπορεύθησαν εἰς τὴν Γαλιλαίαν εἰς τὸ ὄρος οὗ ἐτάξατο αὐτοῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς,
verses.mistral: The eleven disciples went to Galilee to the mountain where Jesus had directed them.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then the eleven disciples went away into Galilee, into a mountain where Jesus had appointed them.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ ἰδόντες αὐτὸν ⸀προσεκύνησαν, οἱ δὲ ἐδίστασαν.
verses.mistral: And falling down they worshiped Him, and saying, they rejoiced.
verses.kjv: And when they saw him, they worshipped him: but some doubted.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ προσελθὼν ὁ Ἰησοῦς ἐλάλησεν αὐτοῖς λέγων· Ἐδόθη μοι πᾶσα ἐξουσία ἐν οὐρανῷ καὶ ἐπὶ ⸀τῆς γῆς·
verses.mistral: Given: And Jesus approaching, He spoke to them, saying, All authority has been given to Me in heaven and on earth.
verses.kjv: And Jesus came and spake unto them, saying, All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: πορευθέντες ⸀οὖν μαθητεύσατε πάντα τὰ ἔθνη, ⸀βαπτίζοντες αὐτοὺς εἰς τὸ ὄνομα τοῦ πατρὸς καὶ τοῦ υἱοῦ καὶ τοῦ ἁγίου πνεύματος,
verses.mistral: Make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: ¶ Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost:

words.gloss: I make disciples, make into disciples

verses.greek: διδάσκοντες αὐτοὺς τηρεῖν πάντα ὅσα ἐνετειλάμην ὑμῖν· καὶ ἰδοὺ ἐγὼ μεθ’ ὑμῶν εἰμι πάσας τὰς ἡμέρας ἕως τῆς συντελείας τοῦ ⸀αἰῶνος.
verses.mistral: Teaching them to observe all that I have commanded you; and behold, I am with you always, to the end of the age.
verses.kjv: Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you: and, lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world. Amen.
